summary: Trace exact code causes for each observed product defect cluster

findings.defect: (a)+(b) Raw '[unverified]' markers spliced INSIDE committed prose strings — including mid-token ('500k [unverified]+ brands', '~149K [unverified]/mo', '4.8K [unverified]/mo', '30–50% [unverified] lower') — plus aggregated footnotes '[N figures in this field are unverified — see section badge]' appended to field text; all recorded as verifierSummary.strippedNumericClaims with action 'marker'/'marker-aggregated'.
findings.rootCause: redactUnsupportedNumericClaims mutates the artifact body itself: markNumericToken does value.replace(token, `${match} [unverified]`) (evidence-support.ts:704) for every numeric claim the substring verifier graded 'unsupported' (collectUnsupportedNumericTokens, :569-599). The marker is written into the SAME string that gets persisted — run-section.ts:2564 commits `body: numericStrip.body`. Three compounding mechanisms: (1) isCleanTokenBoundary (evidence-support.ts:621-665) only rejects splices adjacent to alphanumerics, ',', '.' digit-continuation, and a trailing '%'; it does NOT treat '+', '/', or en-dash as figure continuation, so '500k' inside '500k+' and '149K' inside '149K/mo' and '50%' inside '30–50%' are 'clean' boundaries and the marker lands mid-figure. (2) Above 3 markable occurrences per field (inlineMarkerCapPerField, :714) or past the 6-per-section budget (:721), buildAggregateMarkerFootnote (:727-731) appends the bracketed footnote to the field — text that says 'see section badge' even though the badge chrome was deliberately removed from the reader (no component renders verifierSummary/computedTrust: grep over src/components returns nothing), so the pointer dangles. (3) The reader's compensating scrub (src/components/research-v2/primitives/reader-text.ts:1, TRUST_MARKER_PATTERN /\[(?:unverified|verified[^\]]*)\]/) only matches brackets STARTING with 'unverified'/'verified' — the aggregate footnote starts with a digit ('[4 figures…') so it ships to the client verbatim. Why prose instead of metadata: the metadata already exists (StrippedNumericClaim records carry exact field paths, :382-386), making the inline splice pure duplication; the write-then-unwrite dance (engine splices, reader regex-strips) is the architecture. Downstream consumers that re-read committed bodies (paid-media's ResearchInput merges sibling section bodies; executive brief — see numeric-coherence.ts:12-15 header comment) receive marker-laced prose as model input; live proof: positioningPaidMediaPlan body.audienceTypes[1].grounding contains "4.8K [unverified]/mo".

files.what: token collection, boundary check (misses +, /, – continuations), marker splice into body string, per-field cap + section budget, aggregate footnote builder, top-level redactor
redactUnsupportedNumericClaims invoked inside annotateEvidenceSupportReview; vandalized body committed as the artifact of record
reader scrub strips '[unverified]' but its pattern cannot match the aggregate footnote, and isReaderPipelineChrome has no 'section badge' pattern — footnote ships to client
live evidence: 6 'marker' + 13 'marker-aggregated' records; '500k [unverified]+ brands' mid-token splice

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: Client-deliverable strategy prose reads as if a censor scribbled over it: figures broken mid-token ('500k [unverified]+ brands'), units severed ('149K [unverified]/mo'), and bracketed meta-footnotes telling the client to consult a 'section badge' that no longer exists in the UI. Because the splice happens in the persisted body, every downstream surface (profile, share, executive brief, paid-media planning model input) inherits the vandalism, and the reader can only partially undo it.
findings.smallestFix: Stop mutating body strings: keep recording StrippedNumericClaim {field, value, action} in verifierSummary (already exact-path metadata) and delete the splice + footnote append in appendMarkerActions/markNumericToken; let the reader annotate per-field from verifierSummary if desired. If full removal is too large, minimum patch: (1) delete buildAggregateMarkerFootnote append (the 'see section badge' text is a dead pointer), (2) extend isCleanTokenBoundary to reject '+', '/', '–', '-' continuation after the match. Blast radius: evidence-support.test.ts pins the footnote text verbatim (line 1041) and the marker/marker-aggregated split (lines 1044-1109); run-section verification suites assert needs_review + strippedNumericClaims presence — update those alongside. reader-text.ts scrub can stay as belt-and-braces.

findings.defect: (c) numericCoherenceStrikes false positives: struck the sentence 'Technographically, they use Google Workspace or Microsoft 365…' because '365' tripped the numeric gate, and deleted the BuyerICP verdict's lead sentence (the affirmative ICP definition) over '$1M' in '(10-500 employees, $1M-$100M revenue)'.
findings.rootCause: Four interacting mechanisms in enforceNumericCoherence/gateProseNumbers. (1) extractNumericTokens' bare-integer rule /\b\d{3,}\b/ (numeric-coherence.ts:313-325) treats ANY 3+ digit integer except years 1900-2099 as a falsifiable magnitude claim — product names (Microsoft 365, Fortune 500, S&P 500) have no mask; maskPatterns (:156-162) only covers URLs/citations/24-7/anchors. (2) The truth index excludes all narrative fields (truthExcludedFieldNames :80-85 — prose, strategicVerdict, rationale, recommendation, grounding — 'model may not self-vouch'), so a figure that lives only in narrative can never trace; '365' and '$1M' appear nowhere else in the body (verified: zero other occurrences in the committed BuyerICP body). (3) The gate is fully disconnected from the claim verifier: claims graded 'verified' in artifact.verification are never added to the truth index, so a source-verified figure can still be struck as 'incoherent'. The format-identical fallback (tokenTraceable :545-547) requires rawDigits.length >= 3, so '1M' (length 2) can't be rescued even if present. (4) Strike granularity is the whole sentence (gateProseNumbers :563-579): one false token deletes the entire sentence; for the verdict this ran via gateProseNumbers({field:'verdict'}) at run-section.ts:2487-2491 against sectionTruth (:2469-2473), deleting the verdict's first sentence and leaving the hero leading with 'Wrong-fit buyers are…'. Aggravator: the canonical answer-tool clean-commit call site (run-section.ts:7180-7194) intentionally OMITS researchInput (comment at :2348-2351), so clean sections get a body-only truth universe — stricter than shortfall sections (:7158 passes researchInput), inverting the intended severity ordering.

files.what: truth-excluded narrative fields, masks (no product-name mask), token extractor with bare-int >=100 rule, traceability (incl. length>=3 fallback gap), sentence-level strike, truth index builder
body gating, hero verdict/statusSummary gating against sectionTruth, and the asymmetric call sites (7180 clean path omits researchInput; 7158 shortfall path passes it)
live evidence: '365' strike on icpExistenceCheck.prose; '$1M' strike removed the verdict sentence carrying the ICP definition

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: The gate built to stop fabricated numbers deletes TRUE, useful analysis: the technographic profile of the ICP vanishes because a product is named 'Microsoft 365', and the section's headline verdict loses its first sentence — the actual ICP statement — because a revenue band appeared only in narrative form. The client sees a verdict that starts mid-thought ('Wrong-fit buyers are…') with no affirmative definition, which reads as a broken or lazy deliverable.
findings.smallestFix: Three small, independent patches: (1) add a product-name mask before token extraction (digit preceded by a capitalized word: /\b[A-Z][A-Za-z]+\s+\d{3,4}\b/ masks 'Microsoft 365', 'Fortune 500') or require currency/%/magnitude context for bare 3-digit integers; (2) seed the truth index with claim.value of every verified claim in artifact.verification (cheap union in buildSectionNumericTruth); (3) for hero fields (verdict/statusSummary) excise the untraceable token instead of the sentence (enforceBriefNumericFidelity already does exactly this for brief moves, :857-873 — reuse it). Blast radius: numeric-coherence.test.ts (384 lines) pins sentence-strip behavior and the gap line; wave3-synthesis and run-section streaming tests assert strike counts — run those. None currently pin a Microsoft-365-style case, so the fix needs new pinning tests.

findings.defect: (d) strippedSubjectCtaClaims removed the offer section's best funnel analysis (the 94%-never-convert math, the free-tier paywall insight, the activation-gap diagnosis, and a data-availability red flag) for reason 'contradicted-subject-site-cta' with observedCtas:['free'], pasting the IDENTICAL placeholder sentence into four body fields (strategicInsight.nonObviousRead, singleBindingConstraint.whyBinding, funnelDiagnosis.prose, redFlags.items[1].actualEvidence) — a fifth occurrence appears in review.removedItems where the agentic review itself flagged the placeholder as 'placeholder text, not a substantive finding'.
findings.rootCause: stripContradictedSubjectCtaClaims (source-liveness.ts:1010-1034) walks EVERY string in the body (stripContradictedStrings, :947-1008) and replaces the ENTIRE field with the constant 'Evidence gap: subject-site CTA claim removed…' (:959-960) whenever negativeSelfServePattern (:120-121) matches. That pattern is pure lexical proximity: any negation token (no/not/never/without/lacks/…) within 60 chars of any self-serve keyword (free/trial/signup/sign up/create account/get started/self-serve), in EITHER order, with zero requirement that the sentence claims anything about the subject site's CTA. Verified against each removed string: 'free tier … indefinitely without hitting a paywall' (free→without, branch 2 — the sentence AFFIRMS the free tier, opposite of the gate's target), 'visitor-to-signup, but 94% of those signups never convert' (signup→never — a conversion-rate analysis), 'free users never experience a first-value-moment' (free→never), 'No public breakdown of free vs. paid accounts' (no→free — a data-availability statement). Activation: run-section.ts:2414-2426 enables the gate only for positioningOfferDiagnostic when researchInput is defined, with observations from corpus excerpts + fetched subject pages (collectSubjectSiteObservations :850-877); one anchor text matching freeSignupPattern produced observedCtas:['free'] (:933-945), at which point the whole-body sweep ran. Field-level replacement (vs the sentence-level surgery gateProseNumbers does) is why four multi-sentence analyses became four copies of one placeholder.

files.what: freeSignupPattern + negativeSelfServePattern proximity regex, observedSelfServeCtas, whole-field replacement with constant placeholder, gate entry point
gate wired for offer diagnostic with corpus + step-derived subject-site observations
live evidence: 4 strips with observedCtas:['free'], 5 placeholder occurrences, agentic review flagging the gate's own placeholder

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: The single most valuable analysis in the offer section — the quantified funnel break (3% visitor-to-signup, 94% never convert, constraint sits between first-value and checkout) — is replaced by the same boilerplate apology sentence repeated across the section's strategic-insight, binding-constraint, funnel-diagnosis, and red-flag cards. The client pays for diagnosis and receives four identical 'Evidence gap' stamps; even the product's own review pass recognizes them as placeholder garbage.
findings.smallestFix: Narrow the trigger and the surgery: (1) require an explicit site/CTA claim anchor in the matched window (e.g. the negation must co-occur with website/homepage/pricing page/CTA/path-to-purchase/self-serve MOTION nouns, not just 'free'/'signup'), and (2) strike at sentence granularity (reuse the sentenceSplitPattern approach from numeric-coherence) instead of replacing the whole field, keeping unique per-field remainder text. Blast radius: source-liveness.test.ts (277 lines) pins the placeholder text and current strip shape — extend, don't break, SubjectCtaClaimStrip; offer-diagnostic renderer tests reference field presence only. The gate's true target (model asserting 'no free trial exists' against an observed free CTA) must keep a pinning test.

findings.defect: (e) Liveness/containment row-drops empty out evidence tables while the dependent prose still asserts the dropped numbers: shareOfVoice.slices=[] but shareOfVoice.prose still says 'Smartsheet leads … with 42 creatives … Google — Smartsheet (13); Meta — Notion (15); LinkedIn — Smartsheet and Airtable tied (15 each)'; publicWeaknesses.items=[] under prose claiming 'Three consistent weakness themes'; narrativeArcs.prose says 'Three distinct narrative arcs' over 2 surviving arcs.
findings.rootCause: applySourceLivenessGate (source-liveness.ts:617-777) probes each row's sourceUrl (HEAD/GET, :253-326) and drops rows on http-error/fetch-error/containment-mismatch via dropRowsByPath (:542-589). Two structural gaps: (1) No prose reconciliation exists anywhere — installBlockGapsForEmptiedBlocks (:489-540) only installs a blockGap object on a fully-emptied {prose, rows[]} block, and ONLY for direct children of body (it iterates Object.entries(after) one level deep); it never touches the sibling prose string, and there is no regeneration/rewrite step after drops (annotateEvidenceSupportReview is purely subtractive). (2) The one downstream prose check that could catch the contradiction — enforceNumericCoherence, which runs after the liveness gate (run-section.ts:2403 then 2436) and builds truth from the post-drop body — cannot see these claims: 42, 13, 15 are bare integers BELOW the >=100 floor (numeric-coherence.ts:313-325) and 'Three' is a spelled word only checked inside 'N of M' patterns (:307-311), so the contradicting counts are never extracted as tokens. Aggravator that maximizes drop volume: the model cites bot-walled hub pages as sourceUrls (ads.google.com/transparency 404, facebook.com/ads/library 403, g2.com 403, linkedin.com/ad-library containment-mismatch — all in this run's droppedRows), hosts that ALWAYS fail a server-side fetch with this user-agent, so tool-measured ad-wall evidence is systematically vaporized; the preverifiedUrls exemption (collectPreverifiedSourceUrlsFromSteps :779-820) requires exact-URL equality with a tool-result URL, which hub-page citations don't satisfy.

files.what: blockGap installer (top-level only, never touches prose), row dropper, gate with liveness+containment probing, exact-match preverified exemption
token extractor's bare-integer >=100 floor and spelled-number handling — why post-drop prose contradictions in small counts are invisible
pipeline order: liveness drop → quote dedup → CTA → jargon → numeric coherence; no rewrite step between drop and commit
live evidence: slices=[] + blockGap under prose asserting 42/13/15; items=[] under 'Three weakness themes'; 10 dropped rows, all G2/Meta/Google/LinkedIn hub URLs

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: The client opens a competitor section where every table is an empty husk with a 'rows were removed' apology, directly beneath confident prose quoting the exact numbers those rows carried. It reads as either self-contradiction (numbers with no backing) or data loss — and the strongest competitive evidence (G2 weaknesses, ad-library share-of-voice) is precisely what gets vaporized, because review/ad-library hosts block server-side fetches.
findings.smallestFix: Two-part minimal fix: (1) after dropRowsByPath, when a block lost rows, run the existing sentence-level gate over that block's own prose with the dropped rows' extracted numbers/entities as banned tokens (the containmentNeeds extractor :383-390 already computes them per row) — strikes the now-orphaned sentences and records them as coherence strikes; (2) classify known bot-walled hosts (g2.com, facebook.com/ads/library, linkedin.com/ad-library, ads.google.com/transparency) as un-probeable: skip the probe (containmentChecked=false, keep the row, flag 'unverifiable-host') instead of treating 403 as dead evidence. Blast radius: source-liveness.test.ts pins drop reasons and blockGap shape; competitor-landscape renderer tests pin GapNote rendering for empty walls; computedTrust livenessPassRate/containmentPassRate semantics change if hub hosts stop counting — update deriveWave2TrustConfidence expectations in run-section tests.

findings.defect: (f) Two conflicting confidences per artifact: verifierSummary.computedTrust.confidence vs top-level confidence. Live: positioningPaidMediaPlan top-level confidence = 0.6667 while computedTrust.confidence = 0.4 — the reader displays the higher number.
findings.rootCause: Two computations, one threading bug. Top-level confidence is first set at artifact build (run-section.ts:1019-1022) = deriveGroundedConfidence(verification) = verifiedCount/(verifiedCount+unsupportedCount) (evidence-support.ts:1243-1259; paid media: 20/30 = 0.6667). Inside annotateEvidenceSupportReview, deriveWave2TrustConfidence (run-section.ts:2285-2333) computes the trust number = min(livenessPassRate, containmentPassRate, claimSupportShare) with a 0.4 cap when honestEmptyCore (paid media: min(1,1,0.6667) → capped to 0.4). The intended unification path is trustShortfall: deriveGroundedConfidence returns shortfall.computedTrustConfidence when present (evidence-support.ts:1247-1249). But run-section.ts:2523-2529 only constructs trustShortfall when shortfall !== undefined — for a clean commit (verdict has no evidence shortfall, the common case), trustShortfall stays undefined, so line 2530-2536 recomputes the raw verified-ratio for the top-level field while line 2634-2645 writes trust.confidence into computedTrust. The two numbers are then both persisted and diverge whenever liveness/containment < 1 or honestEmptyCore is true. The reader shows ONLY artifact.confidence (every section renderer passes confidence={artifact.confidence} to VerdictHero — e.g. offer-diagnostic.tsx:146, paid-media-plan.tsx:335; verdict-hero.tsx:28-32 renders it as a 'sourced' chip) and NOTHING renders computedTrust (zero hits in src/components). Aggravator/feedback loop: honestEmptyCore = hasEvidenceGapCore(numericStrip.body) (run-section.ts:2515-2518), and hasEvidenceGapCore (:2248-2265) regex-matches the literal phrase 'evidence gap' in ANY body string — including strings the trust machinery itself injected upstream (the CTA placeholder, numericCoherenceGapLine, TAM 'evidence gap: … unsourced' relabels), so the gates' own vandalism flips the 0.4 cap and widens the divergence.

files.what: initial ratio confidence; hasEvidenceGapCore phrase-match; deriveWave2TrustConfidence; the shortfall-only threading of computedTrustConfidence; computedTrust written into verifierSummary
EvidenceSupportShortfall.computedTrustConfidence and deriveGroundedConfidence's override-then-fallback
reader renders artifact.confidence as a 'sourced' chip; computedTrust is rendered nowhere
live evidence: 0.6666… vs 0.4 in one artifact (honestEmptyCore=true, claimSupportShare=0.6667)

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: The artifact carries two truth scores that disagree by 27 points, and the UI shows the optimistic one — the exact inversion of the trust system's purpose. Anyone auditing the JSON (client export, executive brief, future tooling) sees the contradiction immediately, and which number is 'real' is undecidable from the data. Worse, the honest-empty cap that drives the lower number can be triggered by the pipeline's own injected 'evidence gap' placeholders, so sections get confidence-penalized for being vandalized, not for being wrong.
findings.smallestFix: One-line-class fix at run-section.ts:2523-2529: construct trustShortfall whenever trust !== null, even with no shortfall — `trust === null ? shortfall : { unsupportedLoadBearing: [], issues: [], provenanceFlags: [], ...(shortfall ?? {}), computedTrustConfidence: trust.confidence }` — so deriveGroundedConfidence always returns the single computedTrust number and top-level confidence === computedTrust.confidence by construction. Separately (small, contained): make hasEvidenceGapCore ignore gate-injected strings by checking the pre-gate body (it already receives numericStrip.body; pass the pre-annotation artifact.body instead, or tag injected placeholders). Blast radius: run-section tests assert confidenceChanged/needs_review transitions and exact confidence values in committed envelopes (run-section-artifact-streaming.test.ts, gate suites) — clean-commit fixtures with liveness<1 will shift from ratio to min-based confidence, deliberately; pin the new equality invariant (top-level === computedTrust) in the same change.

clusters.cluster: internal gap machinery shipped as client data

findings.defect: blockGap QA objects (summary/foundCount/requiredCount/sourcingPlan) are part of the CLIENT body contract in every section schema — internal repair bookkeeping is persisted and served as deliverable data
findings.rootCause: There is no internal/external split: the same `body` object is simultaneously (1) the validator contract, (2) the persisted DB row, and (3) the client payload. `evidenceBlockGapSchema` (summary, foundCount, requiredCount, sourcingPlan) is defined in strategic-insight.ts:10-37 and embedded as an optional `blockGap` field on nearly every block of every section body schema (market-category.ts:182-207, voice-of-customer.ts:219-247, demand-intent.ts:107-135, buyer-icp.ts:324-353, offer-diagnostic.ts:91-105, competitor-landscape equivalents). The minimum validators actively FORCE the model to file them: error strings like "have N, need >=5 or body.keywordDemand.blockGap" (demand-intent.ts:442-492, voice-of-customer.ts:481-517, market-category.ts:617/683, buyer-icp.ts:505/545) feed the repair prompt, and every SKILL.md instructs "use blockGap instead of inventing rows" (e.g. positioning-demand-intent/SKILL.md:37,53,65; positioning-buyer-icp/SKILL.md:35,50,62). Code paths also WRITE blockGaps directly into the body: source-liveness.ts:514-536 inserts {summary: "Rows in this block were removed before publishing because their cited sources could not be verified live.", sourcingPlan: ["Re-verify the removed citations..."]} (observed verbatim in positioningCompetitorLandscape.json shareOfVoice + publicWeaknesses), and run-section.ts:1046-1172 writes five blockGaps into the VoC gap body. Verified in run d838ed4e: 14 blockGap objects across 5 section bodies. Egress protection is accidental, not designed: the seven dedicated reader renderers simply never reference blockGap (zero grep hits in section-renderers/), silently dropping it — but the generic fallback renderer (typed-artifact-renderer.tsx:118-161, 408-454) walks ALL body keys and renders blockGap with humanized labels 'Block Gap' / 'Found Count' / 'Sourcing Plan' for any zoneId outside the seven; the share API serves the raw body JSON; and the executive-brief route passes the raw body (blockGap included) into the brief LLM prompt (executive-brief/route.ts:103-147).

files.what: evidenceBlockGapSchema + evidenceBlockGapFieldSchema definition (the canonical blockGap shape embedded in all section bodies)
blockGap embedded on 5 blocks; validators demand 'need >=N or body.X.blockGap', forcing models to file gaps into the client body
code-written blockGap with internal-process summary ('removed before publishing... could not be verified live') — verbatim in run d838ed4e competitor shareOfVoice/publicWeaknesses
buildVoiceOfCustomerBlockGap writes 5 blockGaps into VoC gap body; normalizeVoiceOfCustomerBlockGapOnBlock coerces near-miss blockGap shapes so they parse and commit

ARTIFACT_META_KEYS does not include blockGap; generic walker renders it raw ('Block Gap'/'Found Count'/'Sourcing Plan') for any zoneId not in the 7-case switch; dedicated renderers drop it only by omission
toBriefSectionInput/toSynthesisSectionInput pass the raw body (blockGap, evidenceGap, sourcingPlan included) into LLM prompts for the client-facing brief
skill prompt instructs the model to file blockGap (representative; all six section skills carry the same instruction)

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: A paying client's deliverable contains QA ledger entries: 'foundCount: 0, requiredCount: 3, sourcingPlan: [Re-verify the removed citations against live sources]'. In the JSON/share/export surface and any generic-rendered view this reads as the agency publishing its own incomplete-work tickets inside the report. Even where renderers drop it, the honesty signal is lost entirely (silent hole) instead of being a client-grade gap statement.
findings.smallestFix: Keep blockGap in the validator contract (validators and repair depend on it) but add ONE egress transform at the presentation boundary: a shared presentClientArtifact() that converts each blockGap into a single plain client sentence (reuse clientGapSentence from reader-text.ts) and strips foundCount/requiredCount/sourcingPlan, applied in TypedArtifactRenderer (render blockGap as GapNote instead of dropping), the share route, and the executive-brief section-input builders. Blast radius: read-only transform after validation, so no validator/repair changes; tests pinning current behavior: audit-reader-shell.test.tsx, schema __tests__ that pin blockGap passthrough, run-lab-section/rerun-section route tests.

findings.defect: Code deterministically WRITES 'evidence gap: ...' prefixes into VALUE positions (TAM inputs, reachableRevenueEstimate) and the reader renders them verbatim
findings.rootCause: Commit 11b2bc91 (run d838ed4e rerun-5 fix) made the TAM input status enum `.catch('evidence-gap')` and added a transform that rewrites the input's VALUE: market-category.ts:94-117 `value: \`evidence gap: ${input.value}\`` whenever status is evidence-gap and the value lacks the phrase. Two more code writers do the same: run-section.ts:4005-4019 (pre-parse normalization, same prefix) and evidence-support.ts:850-888 (`evidence gap: ${input.inputType} unsourced` when an unsupported numeric token matches) plus evidence-support.ts:890-921 (prefixes reachableRevenueEstimate). The prefix is a validator sentinel (strategic-insight.ts:167-169 isEvidenceGap exempts such strings from strategic-text rules; required-evidence.ts:62-74 treats them as gap markers), i.e. it exists for MACHINE consumption — but it ships in the client body. On the render side the sentinel is only converted to a client sentence where a renderer happens to call isReaderPipelineChrome/clientGapSentence: market-category.tsx:177-186 does this for reachableRevenueEstimate but renders each TAM input value via scrubReaderText(input.value) (market-category.tsx:190-196), and scrubReaderText (reader-text.ts:27-34) does NOT strip the 'evidence gap:' prefix — only clientGapSentence does. Result observed in run d838ed4e: marketSize.bottomUpTam.inputs[].value = 'evidence gap: keyword volume data not retrieved within available tool budget' shipped verbatim, including the internal 'tool budget' vocabulary the build-prompts reader contract (build-prompts.ts:648) claims is forbidden — that contract is prompt text with no code enforcement on value positions.

files.what: status .catch('evidence-gap') + transform that auto-prefixes input.value with 'evidence gap: ' (commit 11b2bc91)
second deterministic prefix writer in withNormalizedMarketCategoryOutput
verifier relabels TAM input values to 'evidence gap: <inputType> unsourced' and prefixes reachableRevenueEstimate
TamFormulaChain: gap handling only for reachableRevenueEstimate; inputs[].value rendered via scrubReaderText which does not strip the sentinel
scrubReaderText leaves 'evidence gap:' intact; only clientGapSentence/textOrGap convert it — and they are not applied to TAM input values
prompt instructs TAM gap inputs to have value beginning 'evidence gap:' — the sentinel format is mandated end-to-end

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: The TAM card shows raw machine sentinels as data cells: a formula chain whose 'monthly keyword volume' slot literally reads 'evidence gap: keyword volume data not retrieved within available tool budget' — exposing both the sentinel grammar and internal budget mechanics where a number should be.
findings.smallestFix: Render-side: in TamFormulaChain pass each input.value through textOrGap (already imported in market-category.tsx) so sentinel values become GapNote sentences like the estimate already does. The sentinel stays in data (validators/required-evidence depend on it). Blast radius: one renderer function; tests: section-renderers/__tests__/market-category tests and schema __tests__/market-category.test.ts pin the prefix contract (data-side stays, so only renderer tests change).

findings.defect: Model-written 'evidence gap: ...' strings sit in value positions the reader renders raw: competitor verbatimHeroCopy and adPresence estSpend
findings.rootCause: The schemas type these fields as plain z.string().min(1) (competitor-landscape.ts:31 verbatimHeroCopy, :145 estSpend) with only a non-empty check (pushMissingText at :440, :528), and the prompt stack mandates the gap grammar when evidence is missing: build-prompts.ts:182 ('Name the gap... using the format `evidence gap: <missing market evidence>`'), the strategic-text validator error literally instructs 'write exactly `evidence gap: <missing signal>`' (strategic-insight.ts:262-265, echoed in run-section.ts:1286-1313 and offer-diagnostic.ts:243-312 which auto-fill those values in repair), and the competitor SKILL.md says 'write one evidence gap in the relevant block instead of inventing' (positioning-competitor-landscape/SKILL.md:56). So the model dutifully wrote competitorSet.competitors[4].verbatimHeroCopy='evidence gap: no live Coda ad creative retrieved' and estSpend='evidence gap: estimated spend not available... 22 displayable creatives, LinkedIn-heavy' (the second even contains 'displayable creatives' and 'quarantined (identity-unverified)' — vocabulary the jargon gate bans, but only on prose surfaces). The reader renders both raw: competitor-landscape.tsx:552 interpolates estSpend directly ('Ads: {platforms} · {adSignal.estSpend}') with no scrub at all, and :182/:539 feed verbatimHeroCopy into EvidenceChip excerpt unprocessed. The jargon/coherence gate cannot catch it because its surface allowlist is only prose/strategicVerdict for this section (numeric-coherence.ts:60-75) — value cells are explicitly excluded as 'the truth the narrative is checked against'.

files.what: verbatimHeroCopy and estSpend are bare min(1) strings; validator only requires non-empty, so gap-grammar strings pass as data
capability-gap instruction mandating the 'evidence gap: <missing market evidence>' format in section content
estSpend interpolated raw into the Ads line; verbatimHeroCopy passed raw as EvidenceChip excerpt — no isReaderPipelineChrome/textOrGap call
jargon sweep surface allowlist excludes structured value cells, so 'displayable creatives'/'quarantined' in estSpend is never scrubbed
pen preserves 'evidence gap:' sentinels byte-for-byte and assumes 'verification chrome is applied downstream' — false for value positions; no downstream chrome exists there

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: A competitor card's hero-copy slot reads 'evidence gap: no live Coda ad creative retrieved' as if it were Coda's actual homepage copy, and the ad-presence line shows spend as a sentence about quarantined creatives and ad-library tooling. The client sees pipeline excuses formatted as competitive facts.

findings.defect: Paid media channelSuggestions: all four rows shipped recommendation='Evidence gap: channel recommendation missing.' under live verdict chips FIX/KEEP/SCALE/ADD
findings.rootCause: normalizeChannelSuggestion (paid-media-plan.ts:844-865) maps each model row through getString(record.recommendation ?? record.observation, 'Evidence gap: channel recommendation missing.') — any row whose text arrived under a different key (or empty) silently gets the placeholder while channel/verdict/sourceSection survive (verdict is snapped by normalizeChannelVerdict, channel falls back to a hardcoded 4-channel list at :849-854 that exactly matches the four observed rows). getString (paid-media-plan.ts:497-511) substitutes the fallback for anything non-string/empty. Crucially there is ZERO repair pressure: validatePaidMediaPlanMinimums (paid-media-plan.ts:1059-1077) checks ONLY projectedResults for substantive rows (isSubstantiveProjectedResultRow at :1046-1057 exists precisely because 'gap rows must NOT satisfy the floor — otherwise an omitted table can sail through with zero repair pressure'), but no equivalent exists for channelSuggestions, so a 100%-placeholder table commits cleanly. The same getString-placeholder pattern is repeated across ~15 paid-media fields (:667 targeting detail, :683 angle description, :727 hook copy, :747/751 funnel, :772 asset note, :809-816 competitor insights, :831-837 review insights, :878 KPI definition, :891-905 cross-section). The reader then renders the placeholder raw: paid-media-plan.tsx:307 puts `recommendation` in a DataTable column (the jargon gate DOES list 'recommendation' as a paid-media surface, numeric-coherence.ts:64-71, but its patterns at :93-112 match internal vocabulary like CHANNEL POLICY/verifiedCount — 'Evidence gap: channel recommendation missing.' matches none), and paid-media-plan.tsx:164 even composes a keyFinding sentence embedding it: '<channel> is marked FIX: Evidence gap: channel recommendation missing.'

findings.defect: Internal tool names (keyword_volume, web_search, keyword_trends, SpyFu, Firecrawl) ship in client-facing text: blockGap summaries/sourcingPlans and an orderedMoves row that instructs the reader to run our own tools
findings.rootCause: Three converging mechanisms. (1) The prompt EXPLICITLY directs tool diagnostics into shipped fields: build-prompts.ts:182 says 'never name tools... in section prose; tool diagnostics belong ONLY in structured gap fields' — but structured gap fields (blockGap) are part of the committed client body (finding 1), so this rule routes tool names INTO the deliverable (observed: keywordDemand.blockGap.sourcingPlan = ['Run keyword_volume bulk call on 50-100...', 'Use keyword_trends fallback for terms SpyFu cannot measure']). (2) orderedMoves[0].move = 'Run a bulk keyword_volume probe on 50-100 non-branded terms...' (positioningDemandIntent.json) passes every gate: validateOrderedStrategicMovesMinimums → validateStrategicText (strategic-insight.ts:225-266) only checks length>=32/vacuous-pattern/duplication — tool names are not vacuous; the jargon scrub sweeps only prose/strategicVerdict(+rationale for demand-intent) (numeric-coherence.ts:60-75), never `move` or blockGap text; and internalJargonPatterns (numeric-coherence.ts:93-112) contains no tool identifiers at all. (3) The reader's last-line TOOL_NAME_PATTERN (reader-text.ts:4-5) lists searchapi|serpapi|firecrawl|perplexity|brave search|spyfu|answer-tool|lab engine but NOT keyword_volume/web_search/keyword_trends/adlibrary/google_ads/meta_ads/linkedin_ads — and the demand-intent renderer never even consults it for moves: DecisionCard receives move.move and move.rationale raw (demand-intent.tsx:300-308). Also leaking via the same hole: keywordDemand rows ship sourceTitle='SpyFu keyword_volume' and monthlyVolume='143,000 (SpyFu-estimated)' as data cells.

clusters.cluster: Paid media plan — numeric and structural integrity (run d838ed4e, positioningPaidMediaPlan)

findings.defect: (a) Budget cascade does not reconcile: audienceTypes sum $634/day vs campaignOverview $833/day ($634×30=$19,020 vs $25,000/month), campaignPhases sum $20,000 vs $25,000/month, and slot 1 says '60% of search budget' = $300 while the overview prose says 60% of total = $500.
findings.rootCause: The reconciliation contract exists ONLY as prompt text, never as code. section-prompt-guidance.ts:140 instructs the model: 'If you emit monthlyBudgetValue, then dailySpendValue*30, every campaignPhases[].monthlyBudgetValue, and the sum of audienceTypes[].dailyBudgetValue*30 should reconcile to monthlyBudgetValue within $5; otherwise omit the numeric sibling.' DeepSeek violated it and nothing checks: (1) validatePaidMediaPlanMinimums (paid-media-plan.ts:1059-1077) validates exactly one thing — >=1 substantive projectedResults row; (2) the numeric-coherence gate (numeric-coherence.ts:9-15, 60-75) only checks NARRATIVE numbers against structured cells — structured money leaves ($833, $300, $167) are themselves treated as truth, so table-vs-table contradictions are invisible to it (the audience-sum 634 and the leaf 833 are BOTH legal truth values); (3) checkPaidMediaChannelPolicy (channel-policy.ts:259) parses the budget only to compare against per-platform minimums; (4) verifyPaidMediaPlan/extractPlanClaims (claim-source-verifier.ts:431-531) extracts hooks/angles/insights — no budget fields at all and zero arithmetic. The intended contract is also visible in the proof script: dailySpend = monthly/30 (zz-prove-lean-media-plan-v3.ts:71) and audience dailyBudget = daily/3 equal split (:101) — both dropped when the throwaway schema was productionized.

files.what: The $5-tolerance cascade-reconciliation rule — prompt text only, no code twin
validatePaidMediaPlanMinimums — the only paid-media code validator; checks one SOP row, no sums
Gate scope = narrative-vs-evidence only; structured money cells are truth, never cross-checked against each other
extractPlanClaims/deterministicPlanPass — no budget claims, no arithmetic; verifier is advisory (1097-1106)
Intended contract: dailySpend=monthly/30, audience split=daily/3, fixed creative counts — present only in the throwaway prompt
Live evidence: 300+167+167=634 vs dailySpendValue 833; 15000+5000+0=20000 vs monthlyBudgetValue 25000

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: A media buyer reading the deck sees three budget tables that contradict each other and the headline budget — the single fastest way to lose trust in a paid plan. $5,000/month (the 20% brand-defense slice the prose promises) simply vanishes between the overview and the phases, and the daily plan underspends the stated monthly budget by ~$6,000/month with no acknowledgment.
findings.smallestFix: Add a deterministic cascade check in code next to the existing repair machinery: extend validatePaidMediaPlanMinimums (paid-media-plan.ts:1059) to push errors when emitted numeric siblings violate the already-documented $5 rule (audience-sum×30 vs monthly, phase budgets vs monthly, dailySpend×30 vs monthly — phases need a tolerance since overlapping phases are legal; the safest invariant is sum(audience dailyBudgetValue) == dailySpendValue and dailySpendValue×30 ≈ monthlyBudgetValue). validateMinimums already feeds the repair loop (run-section.ts:1385,1429,1643,1768,6890 via section-registry.ts:347), and the repair prompt already carries the rule text, so errors are self-explanatory. Fallback posture for unrepairable mismatch: drop the offending *Value siblings + snap their provenance to 'unknown' (the existing normalizeMoneyValue mechanism at paid-media-plan.ts:533-541 then hides the number) rather than hard-failing. Blast radius: paid-media repair attempts increase; tests pinning current behavior are src/lib/lab-engine/artifacts/schemas/__tests__/paid-media-plan.test.ts (validateMinimums cases) and the section-registry contract test; the renderer (src/components/research-v2/section-renderers/paid-media-plan.tsx:188-190) tolerates missing values already.

findings.defect: (b) '(user-supplied)' provenance attached to numbers the model derived — '$300/day (60% of search budget) (user-supplied)', '$833/day' dailySpendProvenance:'user-supplied' — when only $25,000/month and CAC ≤$4,000 actually came from the brief.
findings.rootCause: Three stacked mechanisms, all verified. (1) Provenance is model-asserted and schema-unchecked: every money provenance field is z.string().min(1) (paid-media-plan.ts:65,68,79,88), and snapMoneyProvenance (390-420) only alias-maps strings into the enum — it never compares the value against onboarding economics. The skill (SKILL.md:32 'Operator-provided money uses user-supplied provenance') relies on the model to self-classify; DeepSeek labeled its own 60/20/20 derivations 'user-supplied'. (2) The redaction pipeline explicitly immunizes model-claimed 'user-supplied': the claim verifier correctly graded $833, $300, $167, $15,000/mo, $5,000/mo as 'unsupported' (artifact verification[] lines 541-548, 564-572, 610-618, 665-682 — no match in readResearchInput), but redactPaidMediaMoneyFields skips any money field whose provenance string is in trustedPaidMediaMoneyProvenances = {user-supplied, tool-measured, source-reported} (evidence-support.ts:402-406, 1032-1039). The skip exists precisely for verifier-UNSUPPORTED tokens — i.e., the model's own enum defeats the verifier's verdict. This is pinned by test ('downgrades untrusted paid-media money provenance without touching user-supplied economics', evidence-support.test.ts:733-765 — whose fixture is literally this bug shape: derived $3,333/day labeled user-supplied on a $99,999 budget). Money display fields are also exempted from inline [unverified] markers (evidence-support.ts:1143-1153), so the only mechanism that could have touched them is the one that was skipped. (3) The '(user-supplied)' text inside the label strings is model-authored duplication that section-prompt-guidance.ts:138 forbids ('Numeric siblings must not duplicate provenance in strings') but nothing strips; the reader then renders the raw label AND a separate chip from the enum (paid-media-plan.tsx:188-190, 349) — provenance shown twice, both wrong. Note: the recent .catch('unattributed') change (paid-media-plan.ts:17-21, 42-43, commit 37b3bfa5) only snaps sourceSection/verdict enums; it is unrelated to money provenance and did not cause this.

Test that PINS the user-supplied immunity (fixture is the exact bug shape)

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: The deliverable tells the client THEY supplied $300/day, $167/day, $15,000/month figures they never gave. That is fabricated attribution wearing the strongest trust badge in the provenance vocabulary — worse than an unmarked estimate, because it shifts authorship of invented numbers onto the client, and the agentic review then launders it further ('(client brief)' throughout review.upgradedMarkdown).
findings.smallestFix: Make 'user-supplied' earn the skip: in redactPaidMediaMoneyFields (evidence-support.ts:1032-1039), only honor a trusted provenance when the matched unsupported token numerically appears in ResearchInput onboarding economics (the runner already has researchInput at the call site via annotateEvidenceSupportReview, run-section.ts:2452-2458; pass a set of brief money values down, or simpler: treat 'user-supplied' like other provenances when the token was verifier-unsupported, downgrading to 'model-estimated' instead of 'unknown' so the display string survives with an honest label). Separately, one-line label hygiene in normalizeCampaignOverview/normalizeAudienceType/normalizeCampaignPhase: strip a trailing '(user-supplied|tool-measured|source-reported|model-estimated|unknown)' parenthetical from display strings — provenance has exactly one writer, the enum. Blast radius: evidence-support.test.ts:733-765 must be rewritten (it currently pins the bug); genuinely brief-supplied numbers that the substring verifier failed to match (formatting drift, e.g. '$25,000' vs '25k') risk downgrade — mitigate with the numeric-match-against-onboarding check rather than the blanket un-skip. Derived-but-honest math ($833=25000/30) should come back as 'model-estimated', which is the truthful label.

findings.defect: (c) projectedResults has no projections: both rows carry kpiCostProvenance:'unknown', no kpiCostValue, no projectedCountValue — only budget echoes plus a dangling marginOfErrorPercent:20.
findings.rootCause: The SOP math EXISTS and is correct but is starved of its input by design. normalizeProjectedResultRow (paid-media-plan.ts:936-994) computes projectedCountValue = Math.floor(phaseMonthlyBudgetValue / kpiCostValue) at lines 959-965, but only when the MODEL emitted kpiCostValue with non-'unknown' provenance (945-948 zero out kpiCost when provenance is 'unknown'). The production prompt actively invites the empty path: build-prompts.ts:879 — 'KPI cost unknown? Set kpiCostProvenance to "unknown" and omit kpiCostValue — the row ships without a count.' Nothing anywhere derives a KPI cost from the data the pipeline already holds: the brief's user-supplied target CAC ≤$4,000 flows into the prompt economics frame (build-prompts.ts:466 'targetCac') but is never mapped to kpiCostValue in code; the CPC/CTR/CVR benchmark machinery that could band a cost-per-signup (auditPaidMediaFeasibility, synthesis/feasibility.ts) is wired ONLY into the executive-brief route (src/app/api/research-v2/executive-brief/route.ts:255) — zero references in run-section.ts — so body.feasibilityAudit (schema-optional, paid-media-plan.ts:312) is absent from the section artifact. Meanwhile marginOfErrorPercent is pinned unconditionally to the SOP constant 20 (lines 934, 991) even when there is no count for the margin to qualify, and the schema makes it required (line 182), producing a ±20% on nothing.

files.what: SOP row schema + normalizeProjectedResultRow: floor(budget/kpiCost) math gated on model-supplied kpiCost; marginOfErrorPercent pinned 20 unconditionally
buildProjectedResultsPromptLines — instructs 'unknown' as the easy out, no instruction to derive kpiCost from brief CAC or feasibility data
CPC/CTR/CVR benchmark + cost-per-conversion machinery that could band a KPI cost

Live evidence: two rows, kpiCostProvenance 'unknown', marginOfErrorPercent 20, only phaseMonthlyBudgetValue echoes

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: A table titled 'Projected Results' that projects nothing — it re-states the budget twice and attaches ±20% to a number that does not exist. The client gave the one number needed to run the math (CAC ≤ $4,000: 15000/4000 → ~3 signups/month ±20%, an honest and useful projection); the pipeline holds it, displays it as the primary KPI two cards up, and still declares cost 'unknown'.
findings.smallestFix: Bridge the brief CAC into the math in code (model never does math, consistent with the Iron Law): in normalizeProjectedResultRow — or better, a runner-side pre-pass in withNormalizedPaidMediaPlanOutput (run-section.ts:4055) which has researchInput in scope via its caller — when kpiCostProvenance is 'unknown' and onboarding.economics.targetCac parses to a number and the row's kpi matches the CAC unit (signup/customer per primaryKpi), set kpiCostValue from targetCac with provenance 'user-supplied'; the existing floor() math and weakest-provenance inheritance (929-932, 982-990) then run unchanged. Also make marginOfErrorPercent honest: move it inside the projectedCountValue-present branch and make it optional in the schema (line 182), with the renderer fallback (paid-media-plan.tsx:240-243) already tolerating absence. Blast radius: normalizePaidMediaPlanBody is currently researchInput-blind — threading the CAC in touches run-section.ts:4055-4077 and its call site; tests pinning current behavior: schemas/__tests__/paid-media-plan.test.ts SOP-row cases (floor math, unknown→no-count) and section-renderers/__tests__/paid-media-plan.test.tsx; the schema-optional change to marginOfErrorPercent must keep parse-compat with already-persisted artifacts (it is read, not re-validated, on render — verify the reader path).

findings.defect: (d) salesProcess is a single boilerplate gap row — and structurally can never be anything else, even when the client uploads sales assets.
findings.rootCause: Two layers. The gap row itself is intended design: normalizeSalesProcessAssets (paid-media-plan.ts:776-796) substitutes one synthetic runner-authored gap object when the model emits no assets, per the skill Iron Law (SKILL.md:42) — honest for THIS run, where no docs were supplied. The real defect is upstream plumbing: onboarding DOES capture sales assets (salesProcessDocs + salesLoomUrl — onboarding-v2-types.ts:60,204; parsed into ResearchInput at corpus-to-research-input.ts:301-324; typed in the envelope at artifact-envelope.ts:115-116), but the prompt builder strips raw onboarding from the ResearchInput JSON (buildSectionScopedResearchInputForPrompt, build-prompts.ts:552-553) and replaces it with onboardingStrategicFrame (buildOnboardingStrategicFrame, build-prompts.ts:422-487), which serializes primaryGoal/segments/offers/channels/constraints/gtmMotion/creativeCapacity/leadListAvailable/economics — and omits salesProcessDocs, salesLoomUrl (and notes) entirely. Verified by reading the full function: no other section prompt path injects them. So the model can never see supplied sales assets; combined with 'Never invent URLs' (SKILL.md:42), the only legal output is the gap row, every run, regardless of input.

The gap-row fallback at paid-media-plan.ts:776-796 stays as-is for genuinely missing assets. (UI polish — rendering the gap as a callout instead of a one-row table — is optional and out of this fix's scope.)

findings.defect: (e) creativeStrategy counts videoCount:9 / staticCount:6 / totalPerAudience:5 are unexplained, ungrounded, and disconnected from the actual creative plan (creativeFramework has 6 slots; prose never mentions counts).
findings.rootCause: These were fixed template constants in the original SaaSLaunch 13-slide deck — the proof script pins them as 'Fixed 5' static / 'Fixed 3' video (UGC) / 'Fixed 8' total per audience (zz-prove-lean-media-plan-v3.ts:132-137) and its prompt bound them explicitly ('staticCount 5, videoCount 3, totalPerAudience 8', :398). Productionization dropped the fixed-ness everywhere: the production SKILL.md (v3.2.0) does not mention creativeStrategy or the counts at all (full file read — only 'creative slots' in the no-padding law); the schema accepts any z.number() with no provenance siblings and no .describe() semantics (paid-media-plan.ts:102-107); section-prompt-guidance.ts:142,148 names the keys and says 'emit those as numbers' without defining what they count; and no validator relates them to creativeFramework.length, audienceTypes.length, or creativeCapacity. So DeepSeek free-invents (9/6/5 — readable as 5-per-audience×3=15=9+6, but contradicting its own 6-slot framework and the SOP's 5/3/8). Two aggravators: normalizeCreativeStrategy (paid-media-plan.ts:691-700) silently INVENTS 5/3/8 when the model omits the fields — fabricated plan numbers with no gap signal; and because these are structured leaves, they enter the numeric-coherence truth index (numeric-coherence.ts builds truth from structured cells), so an invented count could even legitimize matching phantom numbers in prose.

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: The reader shows three production-commitment numbers ('Static creatives: 6, Video creatives: 9, Total per audience: 5') that nothing in the plan explains or supports, and that disagree with the 6 creative slots actually presented. To a media buyer they read as a creative-production quote pulled from thin air; on a run where the model omits them, the client would receive silently fabricated 5/3/8 instead.
findings.smallestFix: Single-writer the counts in code: in withNormalizedPaidMediaPlanOutput / normalizeCreativeStrategy, derive them from the plan itself instead of trusting (or inventing for) the model — e.g. staticCount/videoCount from the SOP capacity policy keyed off onboarding.creativeCapacity (the field already flows into the frame, build-prompts.ts:439-441) or from creativeFramework slot labels, and totalPerAudience = staticCount+videoCount; drop the model's values entirely (mirroring the projectedCount 'model never does the math' posture, and overwriting rather than defaulting kills the silent 5/3/8 fabrication at paid-media-plan.ts:696-698). Alternatively, the truly smallest change: restore the template constants to SKILL.md + add the counts-must-equal-framework consistency error to validatePaidMediaPlanMinimums. Blast radius: small — body.creativeStrategy consumers are the reader renderer (paid-media-plan.tsx) and fixtures (src/lib/lab-engine/fixtures/paid-media-plan-artifact.ts); tests pinning current behavior: schemas/__tests__/paid-media-plan.test.ts (normalizer default cases) and section-renderers/__tests__/paid-media-plan.test.tsx.

clusters.cluster: Post-commit agentic review layer rewriting evidence (upgradedMarkdown / tierRationale / removedItems / clientQuestions)

findings.defect: The post-commit review pass regenerates the ENTIRE client-facing section markdown from a massively truncated view of the artifact, with zero evidence verification on its output — inventing quotes, numbers, and prices (VoC '5 tools to 2', '10 hours a week', G2/TrustRadius success-quote table; Offer '40-60%' benchmark; Competitor $18/$19/$32/$19 price set vs body $10/$12/$25/$10).
findings.rootCause: reviewAndUpgradeSection (agentic-section-review.ts:573-623) is one generateText call (deepseek-v4-flash in prod — models.ts:11,461-505 reviewModel defaults to the section runner model under deepseek-direct; thinking disabled, temp 0.1, maxOutputTokens 8000). buildReviewPrompt (494-530) feeds: (a) 'Current section markdown' = buildOriginalArtifactMarkdown (154-178), a 4-line title/verdict/summary STUB — not a render of the body — so the model must author all section prose from scratch; (b) 'Validated artifact JSON' truncated to MAX_ARTIFACT_JSON_CHARS=24,000 (line 18, 525). Live-verified on run d838ed4e: the Competitor artifact pretty-prints to 267,216 chars and every real price ($10/$12/$25) first appears at char ~125,222 — far beyond the 24k window — so the model reconstructed Notion/Monday/ClickUp/Smartsheet pricing from training priors. The only post-processing is parseSectionReviewResponse (tail parse, 196-218), sanitizeReviewVerifiedMarkers + sanitizeReviewVerificationChrome (bracket stripping, 430-472), and the removedItems filter. The evidence-support verifier, provenance gate, and numeric-coherence gate that police the section BODY pre-commit never run on upgradedMarkdown; sectionReviewResultSchema requires only z.string().min(1) (artifact-envelope.ts:257); assertSectionArtifactPersistable validates envelope/body schema only (section-registry.ts:433-458). The prompt's anti-fabrication rules ('Do not invent market data, prices, competitors, quotes...') are pure prompt text — the exact protection class learned-patterns.md says must be deterministic code.

files.what: Review prompt (stub markdown + 24k-truncated artifact JSON + 12x1200-char corpus excerpts), single generateText call, and the full output path showing no evidence verification
reviewModel = deepseek-v4-flash section runner model under prod deepseek-direct (no LAB_REVIEW_MODEL override)
Post-commit scheduling from saveArtifact; attachAgenticReview only re-validates envelope schema (assertSectionArtifactPersistable) — no content check
Detached review route (ADR-0012): x-internal-key auth + Zod envelope parse only, runs runPostCommitAgenticReview in after() with 90s timeout
Live proof: $18/$19/$32 in upgradedMarkdown, absent from body; real prices at char ~125k of 267k pretty JSON, beyond the 24k prompt window
Live proof: '5 tools to 2', '10 hours a week', and G2/TrustRadius-attributed success quotes not present in body

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: A research product sold on evidence honesty ships a model-authored 'upgraded' deliverable containing fabricated customer quotes with fake G2/TrustRadius attribution, invented benchmarks, and wrong competitor prices that contradict the verified typed cards in the same artifact. Any client who checks one price or one quote loses trust in the whole audit — exactly the fabrication class the body-side truthgate was built to prevent, re-introduced after the gate.
findings.smallestFix: Two-part minimum: (1) gate upgradedMarkdown through the existing deterministic checks before it is accepted — at minimum, run the numeric/quote evidence-support check against the artifact body + corpus and reject (fall back to buildFallbackReview tier='unavailable') on any number/quoted-string not present in body or excerpts; (2) stop feeding a stub — either pass the full body (or per-field summary) instead of formatJson(artifact, 24000), or scope the review to tier/clientQuestions only (see finding 2's one-line alternative which removes the fabrication surface entirely). Blast radius: review/__tests__/agentic-section-review.test.ts (16 tests) pins parse/sanitize/fallback behavior; commit-patch.test.ts pins upgradedMarkdown substitution.

findings.defect: upgradedMarkdown silently REPLACES the committed section's `markdown` column in the DB, so the invented prose becomes the canonical markdown for the share view, chat-edit surface, and any markdown consumer — while the verified body is never touched, leaving two divergent 'truths' persisted per section.
findings.rootCause: buildReviewCommitPatch → buildCommitPatch (commit-patch.ts:109-114): `const markdown = reviewMarkdown ?? markdownLines.join('\n')` where readDisplayableReviewMarkdown (62-71) returns review.upgradedMarkdown for every tier except 'unavailable'/errorDiagnostics — including 'needs_review' and 'insufficient'. runPostCommitAgenticReview (supabase-run-store.ts:651-659) re-commits the section with this patch (markdown=upgradedMarkdown, data=artifact-with-review) and then re-runs the profile fanout with the reviewed artifact (674-682). The body is never mutated: attachAgenticReview does `{...input.artifact, review}` (606-609) — review is log-only relative to body. Rendering audit: the live reader (audit-reader-shell.tsx) renders TypedArtifactRenderer over the BODY and surfaces only review.clientQuestions (lines 316-330, 1286; COPY_META_KEYS at 113-121 excludes review from the copy path) — so the reader does NOT show upgradedMarkdown. But the share snapshot stores the markdown column (share-snapshot.ts:156, 234) and shared-session-view.tsx:212-215 renders it verbatim whenever the typed-artifact parse fails; audit-state/route.ts:586-604 ships it to the client; chat/route.ts:255-292 treats `artifact.markdown` as 'the rendered field the workspace UI displays' and patches it for edit_narrative.

files.what: The substitution: review.upgradedMarkdown becomes the committed section markdown for any displayable tier
Body never mutated ({...artifact, review}); review patch re-commit + profile fanout with the reviewed artifact
Share view renders section.markdown (= upgradedMarkdown) as <pre> whenever pickPositioningTypedArtifact returns null
Live reader renders typed BODY cards, never upgradedMarkdown; only clientQuestions surface; copy path excludes review
edit_narrative patches artifact.markdown — the column now holding upgradedMarkdown
markdown column shipped to the frontend in sectionsByZone

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: The exact surface a user shares with a client (read-only share link) is the one that falls back to the fabricated markdown when typed parsing hiccups — fabrications reach the audience least able to cross-check them. Meanwhile the operator's own reader shows the honest body, so the operator never sees what the client sees. Chat edits also operate on the fabricated text, laundering it further.
findings.smallestFix: One line: in buildCommitPatch, stop substituting reviewMarkdown — always use the deterministic verdict/summary lines (`const markdown = markdownLines.join('\n')`), keeping review metadata (tier, clientQuestions) intact. This makes the review badge/questions-only, removing the fabrication surface without touching the review call. Blast radius: shared-view fallback shows verdict+summary instead of full prose (acceptable: typed renderer is the primary path). Tests pinning current behavior: src/lib/research-v2/__tests__/commit-patch.test.ts (expects patch.markdown === upgradedMarkdown at lines 20-30, 55-69), share-snapshot.test.ts, supabase-run-store.test.ts, shared-session-view.test.tsx, audit-reader-shell.test.tsx.

findings.defect: review.removedItems are theater: they claim removals that never happened (paraphrased text still in body), reference text that never existed in the artifact (including the review prompt's own scaffolding boilerplate and envelope metadata), and the 'honesty' filter is logically inverted so both classes pass — then these phantom removals propagate into profile insights and the synthesis contradiction scanner as authoritative facts.
findings.rootCause: Three verified mechanisms. (1) Inverted filter: enforceReviewRemovedItemsHonesty (agentic-section-review.ts:380-428) drops an entry only when a quoted token IS still found bare in body-JSON+upgradedMarkdown (hasFalseRemovalClaim 358-378); a token found NOWHERE is treated as an honest removal — so claims about never-existing text always pass. Live: VoC removedItems[1] 'Removed confidence: 0.5416666666666666' is the ENVELOPE confidence field (in the prompt's artifact JSON, never in body) — kept. (2) Prompt self-pollution: buildOriginalArtifactMarkdown (154-178) appends '## Review status / The automated review pass was unavailable...' boilerplate to EVERY review prompt's 'Current section markdown', even for committed artifacts; the model dutifully 'removes' it — live: VoC removedItems[0] quotes exactly this scaffolding line. (3) Paraphrase blindness: the check is exact-substring over lowercased whitespace-collapsed JSON.stringify (226-250) — JSON escaping (\" , \n as literal chars) and any rewording defeat it. Live: Competitor removedItems[0] 'Removed Smartsheet runs the largest verified ad presence... — retained as observational finding' (self-contradictory) passed because the full quoted token isn't verbatim in body, yet 'largest verified ad presence' IS still in body. Body is never mutated (supabase-run-store.ts:606-609), so 'removed' can only ever mean 'absent from the upgradedMarkdown rewrite'. Downstream, contradictions.ts:251-280 ingests removedItems as 'stripped claims' for the synthesis contradiction scan, and section-profile-persistence.ts:231-236 persists removedItems + tierRationale into profile insights.

files.what: Scaffolding boilerplate injected into every prompt; normalize/JSON-stringify search text; inverted honesty logic (found-bare = dishonest, absent = honest)
Live proof: removedItems quote the prompt's own 'automated review pass was unavailable' boilerplate and the envelope confidence float — neither ever in body
Live proof: claims removal of Smartsheet ad-presence line while 'largest verified ad presence' remains in body; entry even says 'retained as observational finding'
removedItems consumed as authoritative 'stripped claims' for the cross-section contradiction scan
tierRationale + removedItems + clientQuestions persisted into profile insights

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: The verification chrome itself lies: the artifact asserts removals that didn't happen and removals of things that never existed, so anyone auditing the audit (the user, a judge, the synthesis pass) is fed false provenance about what was cleaned. The synthesis contradiction scanner then treats phantom removals as real stripped claims, corrupting downstream coherence checks.
findings.smallestFix: Make the honesty rule two-sided and deterministic: keep a removal entry only if its quoted token (a) WAS present in the original body/statusSummary (search the raw strings, not JSON.stringify output, to avoid escaping artifacts) AND (b) is absent from upgradedMarkdown. Entries failing (a) are phantom references; entries failing (b) are unapplied removals — drop both. Separately, stop injecting the 'automated review pass was unavailable' Review-status block for non-null artifacts in buildOriginalArtifactMarkdown (agentic-section-review.ts:174-177) so the model can't 'remove' scaffolding. Blast radius: agentic-section-review.test.ts pins the current filter semantics (16 tests, several specifically on relabel-aware removal claims); the wave3-synthesis tests pin removedItems→strippedClaims flow. If finding 2's one-line fix lands (markdown no longer replaced), 'removal' semantics become purely advisory and the simplest correct move is to drop removedItems from client-facing persistence entirely.

findings.defect: The unverified review output steers run-level quality signals: review.tier feeds the section verification badge and the live quality gate, and tierRationale ships unverified meta-claims (e.g. VoC: 'All quotes ... are sourced from public review platforms as documented in the source list' — written by the same model that invented four of those quotes).
findings.rootCause: buildCommitPatch (commit-patch.ts:119-134) merges review.tier into the persisted verificationFlag via buildReviewVerificationFlag (verification-tier.ts:155-180) — strictest-tier-wins, so it can only DOWNGRADE (verified design intent, not a bug), but the downgrade reason is a model opinion formed from the same 24k-truncated view. live-quality-gate.ts reads review tier as gate input (859-884) and emits strike/warning reasons from it (510-517, 1202-1206), and tierRationale persists verbatim to profile insights (section-profile-persistence.ts:233). Nothing validates tierRationale's factual assertions; it is z.string().min(1) (artifact-envelope.ts:259). Live: all three reviewed sections in d838ed4e carry tier='needs_review' with rationales asserting source-grounding the review never checked.

files.what: buildReviewVerificationFlag: review tier merged strictest-wins into the persisted badge
Review tier folded into verificationFlag/verificationTier on the committed section row
Live quality gate consumes review tier for blocking reasons and warnings
Live proof: rationale asserts all quotes are platform-sourced while the same review's upgradedMarkdown invented quotes

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: The run's trust signals (badge tier, gate strikes, profile insight rationale) are partially authored by the least-verified component in the pipeline. A 'needs_review' badge with a rationale that itself fabricates a clean bill of health teaches users to ignore the badges; gate output inherits model noise.
findings.smallestFix: Keep the strictest-wins merge (it cannot upgrade — safe), but stop persisting tierRationale to client-facing/profile surfaces (drop it from section-profile-persistence.ts:233 summary), and have the live quality gate treat review tier as advisory warning only, never a hard reason (live-quality-gate.ts:513-517). Blast radius: live-quality-gate.test.ts and supabase-run-store.test.ts pin gate-reason strings and persisted insight shape; shared-session-view/audit-reader tests are unaffected (rationale already intentionally not rendered per the 2026-06-11 user decision noted at audit-reader-shell.tsx:313-315).

clusters.cluster: Reader UI renders an analyst console instead of a client deliverable (research-v3 Audit Reader)

findings.defect: (a) Section/field headers render as smallcaps-monospace analyst chrome (VERDICT, STRATEGIC INSIGHT, DEPTH GATE, SECOND-ORDER IMPLICATION, NON-OBVIOUS READ, NAMED TENSION, CATEGORY-POWER BET, WHY NOW, EXHIBITS: FULL COMPETITOR COMPARISON)
findings.rootCause: Every heading in the reader is routed through two primitives that hard-code `font-mono text-[11px] uppercase tracking-[0.06em]`: Eyebrow (ui-kit/type.tsx:6-23) and MonoBadge=StatusPill (ui-kit/status-pill.tsx:13-19,49). The label STRINGS are internal schema field names surfaced verbatim: VerdictHero paints 'Verdict' (verdict-hero.tsx:25-27); StrategicInsightPanel paints MonoBadge 'Strategic insight' + Eyebrows 'non-obvious read'/'tension'/'chosen side'/'second-order implication' (strategic-insight-panel.tsx:59,65,74,80,91); market-category passes StrategicField labels 'category-power bet'/'why now'/'risk accepted' (market-category.tsx:375-380); ReaderExhibit prefixes every collapsed table with 'Exhibits: {title}' in the same mono-uppercase trigger (reader-exhibit.tsx:27-36); DataTable th cells use the same class (data-table.tsx:112); SubsectionBlock renders its label as an Eyebrow (subsection-block.tsx:23); the shell adds 'POSITIONING AUDIT' top-bar and 'Section N of 7' Eyebrows (audit-reader-shell.tsx:1185,1235-1237). The exact screenshot strings DEPTH GATE / NAMED TENSION / VERDICT-as-field came from the pre-wave-4 panel (git 48747afc~1 strategic-insight-panel.tsx lines 57-69: Eyebrow 'depth gate', StrategicField labels 'verdict','non-obvious read','second-order implication','named tension'); commit 48747afc renamed some labels but kept the identical mono-uppercase idiom, confirmed live on the current build in tmp/ui-audit/10-fresh-buyericp-top.jpeg (EXHIBITS: FULL COMPETITOR COMPARISON). The generic fallback renderer does the same via humanizeKey() into Eyebrow (typed-artifact-renderer.tsx:97-107,209,348).
