findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: A paying client opens a strategy deliverable and sees debugger-style ALL-CAPS monospace labels naming internal schema fields ('SECOND-ORDER IMPLICATION', 'DEPTH GATE', 'EXHIBITS:') instead of editorial headings. It reads as an engineer's console dump, signaling the document was machine-assembled rather than authored, which directly undermines willingness to pay.
findings.smallestFix: Two-part: (1) restyle Eyebrow once (ui-kit/type.tsx) from mono-uppercase to a sans small-caps/medium editorial label — single component, global visual blast radius across all 7 renderers, governed by DESIGN.md so needs a design decision; (2) replace schema-name label strings at each call site with client language (e.g. 'What this means for you', 'The tension', 'Why now'). Tests pinning current text: section-renderer tests assert some label strings (src/components/research-v2/section-renderers/__tests__/*) and subsection-block.test.tsx; grep for the literal labels before renaming. No data-shape change.

findings.defect: (b) Competitor exhibit table clips text mid-line, stacks the Pricing cell under the Positioning cell, and renders an EMPTY Source column; pricing cells show raw '$10 [unverified]/seat/month… (Plus) — lower per-seat' strings
findings.rootCause: DataTable applies the Tailwind line-clamp class DIRECTLY to the <td> element: wrap:'clamp'|'truncate'|'nowrap' all resolve to CLAMP_CLASS[n] ('line-clamp-3' etc.) put on the td (data-table.tsx:148-174). Tailwind line-clamp sets `display:-webkit-box`, which destroys the cell's `display:table-cell`. Per CSS table fixup, CONSECUTIVE non-table-cell children of a <tr> are wrapped into ONE anonymous table cell — in the competitor columns (competitor-landscape.tsx:399-422) the Positioning (clamp 3) and Pricing (clamp 2) tds are adjacent, so they merge into a single anonymous cell: pricing text stacks under positioning text, the sourceUrl td shifts left under the PRICING header, and the SOURCE header column has no cell at all. Verified visually on the CURRENT build in tmp/ui-audit/10-fresh-buyericp-top.jpeg ('notion.so' link sits under PRICING; SOURCE empty — the artifact's sourceUrl values are valid https URLs, so SourceLink (ui-kit/source.tsx:38-65) is not the culprit). The half-cut 4th line ('automate workflows' sliced horizontally) is the known -webkit-line-clamp + same-element padding artifact: the td carries px-3 py-2.5, so clipped lines paint into the bottom padding box. Separately, raw cell values bypass scrubReaderText: when a column has no render callback, `rendered = rawValue` (data-table.tsx:140-143), so artifact strings containing the '[unverified]' trust marker (verified in tmp/run-d838ed4e/positioningCompetitorLandscape.json competitorSet.competitors[*].pricingPosition: "$10 [unverified]/seat/month (Plus) — …") print verbatim, even though scrubReaderText exists precisely to strip those markers (reader-text.ts:1,27-34). The card view has the same gap at competitor-landscape.tsx:548 (`Pricing: {pricing?.monthlyPrice ?? competitor.pricingPosition}` unscrubbed).

files.what: CLAMP_CLASS line-clamp utilities applied to the <td> itself (breaks table-cell display → anonymous-cell column merge) and default rendering of raw values without scrubReaderText
Competitor exhibit columns: Positioning wrap:'clamp' clampLines:3 and Pricing wrap:'clamp' clampLines:2 are adjacent clamped tds; Source column render is correct but its cell is displaced
Card view prints competitor.pricingPosition without scrubReaderText — '[unverified]' marker leaks here too
scrubReaderText strips [unverified]/[verified*] markers but is never invoked for DataTable default cells
Artifact ground truth: 4 of 5 competitors carry '$N [unverified]/seat/month…' raw strings with valid sourceUrl values

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: The flagship competitor-comparison exhibit looks broken: columns misaligned, text guillotined mid-line, an entire SOURCE column empty (reading as 'no sources exist' when every row HAS a source URL), and internal verifier tokens ('[unverified]') printed inside prices. A client sees a half-rendered spreadsheet with trust markers leaking, destroying confidence in both the data and the product.
findings.smallestFix: In DataTable, move wrapClass (and the clamp) onto an inner <div> inside the td instead of the td itself — td keeps display:table-cell, columns realign, SOURCE cell returns, and clipped lines no longer bleed into padding. Also pass default-rendered string values through scrubReaderText. Blast radius: every table in all 7 renderers (visual only, layout becomes correct); data-table.test.tsx currently pins only headers/numeric/render-callback behavior (no clamp-on-td assertion), so add a regression test asserting the td has no line-clamp class and that a 4-column row produces 4 td cells.

findings.defect: (c) 'UNKNOWN' provenance chips under '$333/day' budget values in the paid-media slot table; current build still leaks pipeline provenance tokens
findings.rootCause: Screenshot 06-paidmedia-mid.jpeg (pre-wave-4 build) traced to the old paid-media renderer: provenanceLabel returned the literal string 'unknown' for missing provenance (git 48747afc~1 paid-media-plan.tsx lines 69-70) and rendered it as <MonoBadge>{provenanceLabel(provenance)}</MonoBadge> directly under each money value (old lines 104-134, 170-175); MonoBadge's uppercase class made it 'UNKNOWN'. Wave-4 (48747afc) replaced the chip text with 'assumption — confirm', but three residues persist at HEAD: (1) provenanceLabel passes ANY unmapped provenance token through raw (paid-media-plan.tsx:91-99 — only undefined/''/'unknown' and 'user-supplied'/'operator-supplied' are translated); (2) formatUsdValue still prints the literal string 'unknown' as a displayed value (paid-media-plan.tsx:123-125), surfaced in FunnelMath KPI-cost steps because the run artifact has kpiCostValue undefined + kpiCostProvenance 'unknown' (tmp/run-d838ed4e/positioningPaidMediaPlan.json projectedResults[0]); (3) the artifact's money strings carry provenance chrome baked into the value text — dailyBudget = "$300/day (60% of search budget) (user-supplied)" — and BudgetBar prints displayValue verbatim (budget-bar.tsx:48-57) while StatCallout shows monthlyBudget "$25,000/month (user-supplied)" (paid-media-plan.tsx:341-366); scrubReaderText never strips parenthesized provenance suffixes.

files.what: provenanceLabel passthrough for unmapped tokens, 'unknown' literal as display value, BudgetBar/StatCallout/FunnelMath fed raw artifact money strings
displayValue printed verbatim, including '(user-supplied)' chrome baked into artifact strings
Ground truth: money strings contain '(user-supplied)' inline; kpiCostProvenance='unknown' with kpiCostValue absent
scrubReaderText strips only [unverified]/[verified*] — parenthesized provenance suffixes pass through

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: Budget numbers — the thing a media buyer reads first — are stamped with raw pipeline state ('UNKNOWN', '(user-supplied)', literal 'unknown' as a dollar value). It reads as the system admitting it doesn't know its own numbers, in debugger vocabulary, exactly where the deliverable needs the most authority.
findings.smallestFix: Make provenanceLabel exhaustive: map every known provenance enum to client language and default unmapped tokens to 'assumption — confirm' instead of passthrough; replace formatUsdValue's 'unknown' with 'not available'; extend scrubReaderText (or a money-specific scrub in paid-media-plan.tsx) to strip trailing '(user-supplied)'/'(operator-supplied)'/'(unknown)' suffixes from displayed money strings. Blast radius: paid-media renderer + reader-text; reader-text.test.ts pins current scrub patterns — extend it in the same change.

findings.defect: (d) Executive brief renders as a wall of undifferentiated small-font paragraphs
findings.rootCause: ExecutiveBriefCard renders the entire executiveThesis (a multi-paragraph AI-authored memo from research_artifacts.thesis) as ONE <Response> block (executive-brief-card.tsx:96-98) under a mono-uppercase 'Executive decision memo' eyebrow (lines 92-94). The vendored Response component's PROSE_CLASS deliberately flattens ALL typography: 14px body AND every h1–h6 forced to text-[14px] font-medium ('so a stray ## can never shout', response.tsx:24-41). Net effect: no lede, no pull-quote, no heading scale, no paragraph differentiation — 5+ dense 14px paragraphs, confirmed in tmp/ui-audit/07-voc-after-wave4.jpeg on the post-wave-4 build. The card is also injected above EVERY section view, always fully expanded (audit-reader-shell.tsx:1227-1232), so the wall precedes each section the user opens.

files.what: Whole thesis rendered as one flattened-markdown Response block; 'Executive decision memo' / 'The Three Moves' mono-uppercase eyebrows
PROSE_CLASS forces all headings to 14px medium — eliminates internal hierarchy of any long memo
Brief card mounted at the top of the reading column for every active section, always expanded

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: The single most important artifact — the executive memo a client would forward to their CEO — has the visual weight of a terms-of-service page. No thesis statement stands out, no skimmable structure exists, and it pushes every section's actual content below the fold on every tab.
findings.smallestFix: Give the brief its own typographic treatment instead of the global Response flattener: render the first paragraph as a lede (larger serif/18px), pass a className override to Response that restores h2/h3 scale within the brief only, and/or collapse the brief after first read. Blast radius: executive-brief-card.tsx only (Response defaults untouched, so chat and narrative prose are unaffected). No test currently pins the brief's typography; audit-reader-shell.test.tsx asserts memo presence — keep green.

findings.defect: (e) VoC body renders raw markdown-link syntax and image-CDN URLs inline ([[airtable 01]](https://legacy-us-images.foundryco.app/...jpg?auto=webp&quality=85,70))
findings.rootCause: The text is ARTIFACT CONTENT, not introduced by rendering: the pre-rerun VoC artifact contained Firecrawl-scraped article markdown (support-article body with image links) inside prose/verbatimText fields, and the pre-wave-4 renderer (git 48747afc~1 voice-of-customer.tsx:167-231, QuoteCallout plain-text blockquote) painted those strings verbatim as plain text — which is why the brackets show literally instead of becoming anchors. The CURRENT saved artifact (tmp/run-d838ed4e/positioningVoiceOfCustomer.json, createdAt 2026-06-11T19:42Z, AFTER screenshot 05's 17:57Z) contains zero markdown links (verified by regex over the JSON), because VoC was rerun. The current render layer added a guard, but it is threshold-based: looksLikeNavMenuGarbage fires only at >1000 chars OR ≥3 markdown links (reader-text.ts:57-60), applied in QuoteCard (quote-card.tsx:31-38), textOrGap in NarrativeBlock (narrative-block.tsx:106) and ExpandableText (voice-of-customer.tsx:147). A field with 1–2 inline markdown/image links under 1000 chars passes the guard and renders the raw bracket syntax, because scrubReaderText/clampReaderText never rewrite '[label](url)' (reader-text.ts:27-34,51-55) and quote/objection text is rendered as plain strings (quote-card.tsx:48, voice-of-customer.tsx:285).

files.what: MARKDOWN_LINK_PATTERN exists only for the ≥3-links nav-garbage heuristic; scrubReaderText never strips/rewrites markdown link syntax
Quote rendered as plain text after clampReaderText — sub-threshold markdown links render raw
ExpandableText/objection text plain-string rendering; usable/unusable quote split relies solely on the nav-garbage threshold
Current (rerun) artifact verified clean of markdown links — proving the leaked text was earlier artifact content, not a renderer fabrication

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: The 'voice of customer' — supposedly verbatim human language — shows scraper plumbing: bracket syntax, CDN query strings, image URLs. It exposes that quotes are scraped page dumps rather than curated customer evidence, the single fastest way to lose trust in the section.
findings.smallestFix: Render-side: extend scrubReaderText to rewrite '[label](url)' → 'label' and drop image-only links, so sub-threshold leaks can never paint raw syntax in plain-text contexts (quote cards, objections, ExpandableText). Root fix is upstream quote admission (other cluster). Blast radius: reader-text.ts is shared by all renderers — reader-text.test.ts pins current scrub outputs and must be extended; keep the ≥3-link nav-garbage gate unchanged.

findings.defect: (f) Right sidebar shows '6/6 Done' and per-section 'Complete' with green checks while every section is needs_review/insufficient; verification_tier/reviewTier never surfaces anywhere in the UI
findings.rootCause: statusOf in the shell derives section status PURELY from job lifecycle: worker.status==='complete' OR mere presence of a section body → 'complete' (audit-reader-shell.tsx:916-935). sectionStatusSubline maps 'complete' → 'Complete' (lines 337-348) and STATUS_META.complete is a green Check labeled 'Complete' (ui-kit/status.tsx:42). The rollup header counts the same statuses into 'N/6' + bare 'Done' (RunStatusCard, lines 638-655). clusters.cluster: research acquisition ceilings that cap evidence quality

findings.defect: (a-1) VoC quotes cite review-site INDEX pages (g2.com/products/airtable/reviews, capterra.com/p/146652/Airtable/reviews/) instead of per-review permalinks, yet ship as 'verbatimText' with no downgrade
findings.rootCause: Three stacked mechanisms, all verified in code. (1) The reviews tool's W5 permalink resolver explicitly falls back to the scraped page URL when no per-review anchor is found within 6,000 chars of the parsed block — Firecrawl's onlyMainContent markdown of Capterra/G2 index pages rarely contains per-review anchor links, so resolvePermalink() returns null and the excerpt keeps the index URL ('honest but vague' by the code's own comment). (2) The quote-admission gate's isPermalinkishUrl() is loose: permalinkPathPattern matches the bare word '/reviews' in a path, and ANY trusted-host URL (g2.com, capterra.com, trustpilot.com...) with >=1 path segment passes — so index pages are admitted as 'permalinkish'. (3) The strict per-review-permalink downgrade (downgradeUnpermalinkedVerbatimQuotes, with correct patterns like capterra /p/\d+/name/reviews/\d+ and g2 review-\d+) runs ONLY for positioningCompetitorLandscape publicWeaknesses — never on VoC painLanguage/successLanguage/objections. The prepass candidate pack also strips 'www.' from candidate URLs (normalizeCandidateUrl -> normalizeHostname), which is exactly why the artifact's URLs are www-less — the model faithfully copied the candidate-pack index URLs. The W5 memory of '7 real G2 review permalinks tier=verified' was NOT a code path that regressed: the machinery is intact; permalink yield is subject-dependent (it requires the SERP to return per-review permalink hits, which get scrape priority -1, or the scraped markdown to carry permalink anchors — neither happened for Airtable's G2/Capterra pages this run; the lone G2 quote verified against perplexity_research step 9, suggesting G2 scrape was blocked entirely).

files.what: W5 permalink resolver: strict isReviewPermalinkUrl patterns (159-170), buildReviewPermalinkResolver returns null past 6k-char anchor distance (177-208), withReviewPermalink keeps the index page URL on null (211-218); comment at 174-176 documents the index-page fallback as deliberate
isPermalinkishUrl passes index pages: permalinkPathPattern matches '/reviews' (40-41), trusted hosts pass with any >=1-segment path (113-117)

verbatimCapableUrlPatterns are the CORRECT strict per-review patterns; downgradeUnpermalinkedVerbatimQuotes prefixes 'Paraphrased pattern (no per-review permalink): ' — but only reads body.publicWeaknesses
downgradeUnpermalinkedVerbatimQuotes gated to sectionId === 'positioningCompetitorLandscape' only; VoC bypasses it
normalizeCandidateUrl strips www. (via domain-utils normalizeHostname:39-47) — produces the exact www-less index URLs seen in the artifact's quote rows

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: A copywriter clicking a quote's source lands on a 50-review index page and cannot find the quoted review — the quote is unverifiable in practice. Worse, the section labels it verbatimText with platform attribution, projecting a precision the link cannot back. This is the exact 'index-page quotes laundered into shippable copy' class flagged on run 8081e646.
findings.smallestFix: Generalize the existing provenance-gate downgrade: run downgradeUnpermalinkedVerbatimQuotes' logic (verbatimCapableUrlPatterns) over VoC quote-bearing arrays (painLanguage.quotes, successLanguage.quotes, decisionCriteria.criteria evidenceQuote) in run-section.ts:2384, so index-page-cited verbatimText is relabeled 'Paraphrased pattern (no per-review permalink): ...' — quote survives, honesty restored, no acquisition change needed. Blast radius: VoC schema validators count quotes regardless of prefix (verify VOC_MIN_QUOTES still passes with prefixed text); pinned tests: provenance-gate tests (__tests__), run-section streaming tests, voice-of-customer schema tests. A deeper fix (raising actual permalink yield, e.g. G2 survey_responses SERP query variant) is acquisition work, separate change.

findings.defect: (a-2) Two different Capterra product IDs (146652 and 147768) both claimed as Airtable reviews; 147768 is graded unsupported/no_match by the verifier yet ships anyway
findings.rootCause: Two distinct mechanisms. (1) No product-identity validation anywhere in the reviews path: the SERP query is brand-text ('Airtable <category> reviews complaints ... site:capterra.com OR site:g2.com...', reviews.ts:1227-1230 + prepass brand variants run-section.ts:6048-6068) and deriveReviewSource (reviews.ts:138-152) classifies by domain only — any Capterra product page Google returns for the brand text is scraped and its excerpts attributed to the brand. (2) The run's verification proves 146652 was tool-observed (matched firecrawl stepIndex 13) but 147768 appears in NO tool input/output (claim kind 'url', status unsupported, reason no_match) — i.e. the model mutated/fabricated the second product-ID URL. The machinery DETECTS this (claim-extractor.ts:262 extracts url claims; structural verifier grades no_match) but nothing acts on a fabricated URL row: stripPlaceholderSourceUrls only catches placeholder shapes (example.com, pseudo-permalinks), and the source-liveness gate either skips preverified URLs or probes with a HEAD that a real-host wrong-ID URL can pass. The row ships; the only consequence is the section-level 'insufficient' badge.

files.what: deriveReviewSource is domain-only; brand-text SERP query with no check that the scraped Capterra/G2 page is the subject's own product listing
prepass review queries are brand+category text; stripPlaceholderSourceUrls covers placeholder URL shapes only, not real-host fabricated IDs
capterra /p/146652/ = verified (firecrawl step 13); /p/147768/ = unsupported/no_match — fabricated; both ship in body + sources[]

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: The deliverable asserts two different Capterra product listings are both 'Airtable' — at least one is wrong-product or invented. A client who checks loses trust in every other citation; this is the wrong-company class (Notion->'Notion Limited' book ads) recurring in the review-quote path.
findings.smallestFix: Act on the already-computed signal: in annotateEvidenceSupportReview, when a quote/objection/story row's sourceUrl has a url-claim graded unsupported/no_match (the model authored a URL no tool ever saw), relabel that row's sourceUrl to the evidence-gap marker (same pattern as stripPlaceholderSourceUrls) instead of shipping it. Zero new acquisition; uses verification already attached to the artifact. Blast radius: rows keep their text, lose the fake link; tests pinning current behavior: evidence-support/structural-verifier tests and placeholder-URL tests. Optional second seam (cheap): in reviews.ts, drop Capterra/G2 excerpts whose product-path segment fails a normalized-brand match against the queried brand.

findings.defect: (a-3) VoC quote source enum lacks capterra/trustpilot/trustradius — every Capterra/TrustRadius quote ships as source:'other' even when the platform is known
findings.rootCause: vocSourceTypes is a closed enum ['g2','reddit','hackernews','sales-call','support-thread','twitter','other'] (voice-of-customer.ts:23-31) used by painQuoteSchema and successQuoteSchema (lines 48, 103). The prompt guidance hard-codes the same list (section-prompt-guidance.ts:77), so the model is INSTRUCTED to write 'other' for Capterra/TrustRadius/Trustpilot quotes. The misattribution relabeler comment (run-section.ts:2237-2246) confirms the bind: because the enum is closed, ''other' is the only schema-legal honest relabel' — the schema cannot express the platform even when the sourceUrl proves it.

findings.defect: (b) SpyFu keyword rows cite https://www.spyfu.com/ homepage; 3 of 4 CPCs are 'n/a'; only 4 branded terms measured against a self-declared need of 10+, and the bulk expansion / keyword_trends fallback never ran in-run
findings.rootCause: Four verified mechanisms. (1) Homepage citation is BY DESIGN: SPYFU_SOURCE_URL='https://www.spyfu.com/' is pinned via z.literal in the tool output schema as the W5 provenance bridge (so the structural verifier can match body sourceUrl to a toolResult URL); there is no per-keyword SpyFu URL anywhere. (2) CPC 'n/a' is honest normalization: SpyFu returns $0.00 when it has no auction data; the tool maps cpc<=0 -> null -> display 'n/a' (keyword-volume.ts:54-57, 96); the client maps all v2 CPC field variants (spyfu-client.ts:356), so this is genuine SpyFu data absence for low-commercial branded terms, not a parsing bug. (3) One-shot measurement: verification shows exactly ONE keyword_volume call (stepIndex 0). The shared prompt contract forbids retrying the same tool with different inputs unless the gap reason is rate_limited (build-prompts.ts:181) — a SUCCESSFUL-but-sparse result (4 rows back) is not a gap at all, so the 50-100-term bulk expansion the blockGap itself prescribes is prompt-forbidden in-run; the guidance says 'in ONE bulk call' (section-prompt-guidance.ts:97), reinforcing one-shot behavior. getKeywordsByBulkSearch supports 100 keywords/call (spyfu-client.ts:586-600) — capacity exists, the loop never uses it twice. (4) The validator's escape hatch accepts it: demand-intent requires >=5 keyword rows OR a blockGap (demand-intent.ts:439-443), and the keyword-provenance guard (229-328) correctly strips unmeasured rows — so 4 measured rows + a model-authored blockGap (foundCount 4, requiredCount 10 are model-written fields) commits cleanly, and keyword_trends (in allowedTools, registry line 281) is deferred to a 'sourcingPlan' for a future run nobody executes. Budget pressure compounds it: maxExternalLookups=7 shared across 6 tools (section-registry.ts:285). Unlike the competitor ad probe and VoC prepass, there is NO deterministic keyword prepass that expands candidates (brief competitors + category problem phrases) and bulk-measures them in code.

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: The 'Demand & Intent' section's core promise — measured non-branded demand — is unfulfilled: the entire problem-aware and competitor-alternative surface ('notion alternatives', 'clickup alternatives', 'customer research tool') ships as an IOU ('a bulk call would close this gap') in a paid deliverable, while every row that did ship cites the SpyFu homepage, which a client cannot use to spot-check a single number. Strategy ('capture competitor-alternative searchers via paid search') rests on volumes the run never measured.
findings.smallestFix: Two-part, both small. (1) Carve keyword_volume out of the no-retry rule: amend build-prompts.ts:181 to exempt bulk-measurement tools ('keyword_volume may be called a second time with an EXPANDED keyword list when the first call returned fewer rows than requested'), and change guidance line 97 from 'in ONE bulk call' to 'bulk calls (up to 100 terms each)'. (2) Give rows a navigable citation without breaking the W5 verifier bridge: add a per-row display URL (e.g. https://www.spyfu.com/keyword/overview?query=<kw>) as a SEPARATE optional field while keeping sourceUrl literal-pinned — the verifier match stays intact. Bigger but most durable (note, larger blast radius): a deterministic keyword prepass mirroring the ad probe — expand candidates from brief competitors + category phrases, one bulk SpyFu call + keyword_trends for misses, injected like the candidate pack. Tests pinning current behavior: keyword-volume tool tests, demand-intent schema provenance tests, build-prompts.test.ts (pins the retry-rule string), section-registry contract test if allowedTools change (they don't).

findings.defect: (c) DemandIntent 'job-posting' intent signals are sourced to the subject's OWN marketing pages (blog.airtable.com, airtable.com/ai-plays) — vendor self-sourcing presented as third-party demand signals
findings.rootCause: The skill names the signal class but never constrains its sourcing: positioning-demand-intent/SKILL.md Move 5 (line 53) says intent signals 'show non-search demand: job postings, RFPs, news triggers...' with zero independence requirement — nothing says a job-posting signal must cite an actual job posting on an independent surface. The prompt guidance (section-prompt-guidance.ts:104-105) defines only keys and the signalType enum. The validator (demand-intent.ts:478-482) checks only count>=5-or-blockGap — there is NO subject-domain independence check on intentSignals.items[].sourceUrl, in direct contrast to VoC, which has exactly this gate in two places (schema: pain quotes from the subject's registrable domain are validation errors, voice-of-customer.ts:541-565; prepass: subject-domain candidates rejected outright, voice-of-customer-candidates.ts:286-288). Aggravator verified in the artifact: the section's evidence diet is subject-heavy — 6 of 11 sources are airtable.com pages — so the model 'sourced' its hiring-trend inference to the nearest available corpus excerpt, which was Airtable's own content marketing.

files.what: Move 5 lists 'job postings' as an intent-signal type with no independent-source requirement

only count-or-blockGap validation on intentSignals; no subject-domain check

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: The section claims market-side demand evidence ('companies hiring UX researchers need Airtable') but the citation is Airtable telling you Airtable is useful — circular vendor self-sourcing dressed as an intent signal. Any skeptical reader who clicks discovers the 'evidence' is the subject's own blog, which poisons trust in the genuinely-measured rows next to it.
findings.smallestFix: Mirror the VoC independence gate: in validateDemandIntentMinimums, reject (or relabel to blockGap) intentSignals.items whose sourceUrl registrable domain equals the subject's — same getRegistrableDomain comparison as voice-of-customer.ts:541-565, ~15 lines. Add one Iron Law line to SKILL.md Move 5: 'Intent signals must cite independent venues (job boards, news, funding databases) — never the subject company's own domain.' Blast radius: demand-intent artifacts citing subject-domain news (e.g. a real airtable.com press release as news-trigger) would now be rejected — decide whether news-trigger is exempt; pinned tests: demand-intent schema tests + the repair-loop tests that feed validator errors back to the model.

findings.defect: (d) G2/Capterra/Trustpilot 403 at verify time: the source-liveness verifier uses raw fetch with a bot UA (not Firecrawl), so anti-bot review platforms 403 honest rows into drops, while a preverification hole exempts model-fetched URLs from any probe at all
findings.rootCause: Asymmetric fetch stacks, both verified. ACQUISITION uses Firecrawl /v2/scrape with proxy:'auto', waitFor, blockAds (reviews.ts:1046-1067) — engineered to beat anti-bot. VERIFICATION (applySourceLivenessGate) uses plain fetch HEAD-then-GET with User-Agent 'AI-GOS-Source-Liveness/1.0' and a 5s timeout (source-liveness.ts:72-76, 253-326) — exactly what G2/Capterra/Trustpilot block. Proven in the run: DemandIntent verifierSummary.sourceLiveness.droppedRows[0] = questionMining.questions[4] dropped for 'HTTP 403' on https://www.trustpilot.com/review/airtable.com — a real, checkable citation killed because the verifier's fetch is bot-blocked. Meanwhile the preverification set is built from BOTH tool outputs AND tool INPUTS (collectPreverifiedSourceUrlsFromSteps visits toolResult.input at source-liveness.ts:814), so any URL the model itself passed to firecrawl is exempt from probing — VoC's quote URLs (which the model firecrawled per guidance) skipped liveness entirely, which is why the VoC artifact carries NO verifierSummary at all: annotateEvidenceSupportReview early-returns the untouched artifact when zero strikes occur (run-section.ts:2541-2560). Net effect: honest rows on bot-hostile hosts get dropped; model-fetched (and even one fabricated-sibling) URLs sail through. The W5 'G2 review permalinks tier=verified' result did not regress in code — reviews.ts machinery is intact; it is subject- and anti-bot-dependent (see finding a-1).

files.what: bot UA + HEAD/GET raw fetch; non-ok status -> 'dead' -> row dropped (http-error); preverified set includes tool INPUTS (814)
acquisition stack: Firecrawl scrape with proxy auto — succeeds where the verifier 403s; reddit JSON fallback exists for reddit only (943-1033)
liveness gate invocation with preverifiedSourceUrls from modelSteps; zero-strike early return = no verifierSummary persisted (VoC this run)
Trustpilot HTTP 403 drop + two containment-mismatch drops; livenessPassRate 0.9 fed into computedTrust confidence 0.6

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: The verifier inverts trust: a genuine Trustpilot citation is deleted from the deliverable (row dropped, gap installed, confidence docked) because Trustpilot blocks bots, while URLs the model fetched itself — including index pages — are never re-checked. Sections lose real evidence at exactly the hosts where buyer evidence lives (G2/Capterra/Trustpilot), reinforcing the index-page/'other' degradation from findings a-1/a-3.
findings.smallestFix: Treat anti-bot statuses from known review platforms as 'liveness unknown', not 'dead': in probeUrl/applySourceLivenessGate, when status is 403/429/503 AND the host is in a small known-bot-hostile set (g2.com, capterra.com, trustpilot.com, trustradius.com, reddit.com), keep the row, mark livenessPassed:false-but-unchecked, and exclude it from the drop path and passRate denominator (mirroring the existing networkUnavailable carve-out at 698-706). ~20 lines in source-liveness.ts. Larger option (more cost): route probes for those hosts through Firecrawl. Separately consider removing toolResult.input from the preverified set (it lets the model self-vouch) — but that widens probe volume and interacts with finding a-2's fix, so do it as its own change. Pinned tests: source-liveness tests cover drop reasons and pass rates; trust-confidence derivation tests pin livenessPassRate math.

findings.defect: (e) CONTRAST — competitor ad evidence ships per-creative permalinks (adstransparency.google.com/advertiser/AR.../creative/CR...) at good quality; what that path does differently
findings.rootCause: The ad path is single-writer code-owned end to end, which is exactly what the VoC/keyword paths lack. (1) URLs originate from structured ad-library APIs: competitor-ad-adapter.ts:430 sets sourceUrl = rawAd.detailsUrl ?? rawAd.url — the per-creative permalink comes from the API response, never from model text. (2) The model cannot degrade them: withNormalizedCompetitorAdEvidence (run-section.ts:3025-3080) REPLACES body.adEvidence wholesale with code-built advertiserGroups from the deterministic probe; the model contributes only prose, and even prose falls back to a deterministic summary when no verified creative exists. (3) A dedicated additive budget pool (adReservedLookups: 24, section-registry.ts:191-199) guarantees the probe covers the full competitor set instead of rationing against generic lookups — the precise inverse of demand-intent's 7-shared-lookups starvation. (4) Identity is verified in code (domainCorroborated gating from the 2026-06-04 wrong-company fix) before anything reaches the verified wall. Pattern to replicate: evidence acquired deterministically, URLs copied by code not model, reserved budget, identity checks.

files.what: sourceUrl/detailsUrl mapped straight from the ad-library API payload — per-creative permalinks are data, not model output
withNormalizedCompetitorAdEvidence: code overwrites body.adEvidence with deterministic groups; model prose falls back to deterministic summary without verified evidence
adReservedLookups: 24 additive pool — ad acquisition never starved by generic budget
live proof: creative-level adstransparency permalinks per advertiser group

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: None — this is the quality benchmark inside the same run. Its existence makes the VoC/keyword gap diagnosable as architectural (model-authored provenance vs code-owned provenance), not as a data-availability problem.
findings.smallestFix: No fix needed here; use as the template. The portable pattern for the other findings: (VoC) code rewrites quote sourceUrl to the tool-excerpt URL it matched at verification time instead of trusting model-copied URLs; (keywords) a deterministic bulk-measure prepass with its own reserved budget, results injected by code. Each is its own change with its own tests; do not bundle.

clusters.cluster: gates that never gate + status chrome that overclaims

findings.defect: Commit path hardcodes status='complete' for every committed section; verification tiers (needs_review/insufficient) are display-only metadata that cannot block, downgrade, or even change the persisted status.
findings.rootCause: buildCommitPatch unconditionally returns `status: 'complete'` (commit-patch.ts:137) for any artifact that survives the runner. The tier is computed alongside it (lines 115-134) and written into the nullable verification_tier/verification_flag columns, which the DB migration itself documents as 'Display-only honesty metadata' (20260604 migration, lines 1-15, comments at 11-15). The committable-gate's `evidenceShortfall` verdict still carries a `committableArtifact` (committable-gate.ts:141-147), and both runner paths commit it after only annotating a needs_review badge (run-section.ts:5049-5070 for the agent path, 7155-7176 for the answer-tool path; badge set at run-section.ts:2562-2568 `needs_review: true`). The only verdicts that can actually stop a commit are: requiredEvidenceMissing (committable-gate.ts:99-106 → run-section.ts:8234-8266 throw), minimumsFailed WITHOUT a gap-artifact escape (run-section.ts:8204-8232 substitutes an honest gap artifact for VoC/BuyerICP/Competitor/Offer, which then commits as 'complete' too), evidence-gate count exceeding LAB_VERIFIER_MAX_UNSUPPORTED (dead — see next finding), paid-media verifier hardFail (run-section.ts:9636-9668), and a runner throw/timeout which lab-section-job.ts:104-114 + 223-254 converts to markSectionFailed (the only path to a non-complete row). Verified live: all 7 sections in run d838ed4e committed status=complete with tiers needs_review/insufficient (tmp/run-d838ed4e/_manifest.json).

files.what: buildCommitPatch — line 137 hardcodes status:'complete'; tier computed at 115-134 but only into verificationTier/verificationFlag fields
evidenceShortfall verdict still returns committableArtifact; nothing in the gate maps shortfall → non-commit
both shortfall paths commit via annotateEvidenceSupportReview (badge only); gap-artifact substitution; the few genuinely-throwing verdicts
columns declared display-only; commit RPC writes them and calls rollup whenever patch status='complete'
the only non-complete terminal path: runner throw → markSectionFailed

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: A run where 0 of 7 sections cleared 'verified' (two below 50% claim support) renders identically to a perfect run: every section says complete, the parent says complete, and the deliverable ships to the profile. The honest tier the pipeline already computed is invisible at every decision point downstream, so the user is told a 46%-supported market analysis is done research.
findings.smallestFix: Do not block commits (that resurrects the repair-storm/stranded-run failure mode the ARI decision explicitly retired). Instead make the tier load-bearing at read time: keep status='complete' as the orchestration ratchet, but have buildCommitPatch derive a user-facing 'completion grade' from effectiveFlag.tier into the patch, and require every consumer of status (rollup display, reader, profile persist payload) to read the pair (status, tier) instead of status alone. Blast radius: commit-patch.test.ts and supabase-run-store.test.ts pin the current patch shape; the profile-persist CAS (supabase-run-store.ts:931-953) filters on status='complete' and must NOT change. The ARI commit-with-honest-badge invariant in evidence-support.test.ts pins never-hard-fail behavior — fix must not violate it.

findings.defect: The single claim-support gate defaults to Infinity, and the repair trigger is tied to the same number — so with LAB_VERIFIER_MAX_UNSUPPORTED unset (the default), unsupported load-bearing claims neither fail a section NOR trigger a single grounding repair attempt.
findings.rootCause: defaultMaxUnsupportedAllowed = Infinity (evidence-support.ts:43; getMaxUnsupportedAllowed at 1219-1235 returns it for unset/empty/invalid env). getEvidenceGateFailureReason (run-section.ts:2203-2214) returns null when unsupportedCount <= Infinity, i.e. always. shouldRepairAttempt (run-section.ts:2754-2762, with the deliberate-coupling comment at 2743-2753) repairs only when the artifact is null OR that same gate fails — so a parseable artifact with 18/48 unsupported claims (BuyerICP in run d838ed4e) skips repairs entirely and commits on attempt one. All three hard-fail sites (run-section.ts:8269-8302, 9071-9102 area, 9467-9498, 9596-9627) are dead code under the default.

files.what: Infinity default and env parse; comment explains the deliberate default-open choice
gate check, repair trigger coupled to the same ceiling, and the (dead) hard-fail site

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: Sections ship with 27-54% of verifier-extracted claims unsupported and the system never even spends one repair round trying to ground them — the 0.46-0.79 confidence band the user sees in this run is partly the direct product of zero repair pressure, not just thin evidence.
findings.smallestFix: Decouple the repair trigger from the hard-fail ceiling: add a finite repair threshold (e.g. repair when unsupportedLoadBearing > N, N small) inside shouldRepairAttempt while leaving getMaxUnsupportedAllowed/hard-fail at Infinity. Blast radius: bounded — answerToolMaxRepairAttempts already caps cost; the 285s job deadline (lab-section-dispatch.ts:25) and the existing deadline-aware repair-skip (run-section.ts:8180-8198) bound wall time. Tests pinning current behavior: evidence-support.test.ts pins the Infinity default; run-section repair tests pin 'no repair when within threshold' — both need explicit updates, and the 2743-2753 comment documents why presence-based triggering was removed (repair storm), so the new threshold must stay count-based, not presence-based.

findings.defect: Rollup ('6/6') and parent status 'complete' count only rows with status='complete' AND counts_toward_rollup — tiers never enter the SQL, so the ratchet to complete is unconditional once six sections commit anything, including pure evidence-gap artifacts.
findings.rootCause: roll_up_research_artifact (migration 20260603, lines 135-163) counts `status = 'complete' and counts_toward_rollup = true` and ratchets research_artifacts.status to 'complete' when count >= children_total. It is PERFORMed synchronously inside commit_artifact_section whenever the patch status is 'complete' (migration 20260604, lines 100-102 and 145-147) — which is every commit, per finding 1. counts_toward_rollup is purely zone membership: seed_orchestration sets it false only for positioningSynthesis/positioningPaidMediaPlan (20260603 lines 103, 115). children_total comes from the orchestrate fan-out's six zones, so 6/6 means '6 rows wrote something', verified in the manifest (rollup 6/6, parent complete, paid-media counts_toward_rollup=false). The profile-persist claim then keys off the ratcheted parent status (supabase-run-store.ts:931-953 `.eq('status','complete')`), so persistence to the user's profile is also tier-blind.

files.what: counts_toward_rollup assignment and the tier-blind rollup function
rollup invoked on every status='complete' commit
profile-persist CAS keyed on parent status='complete'; documented read-after-write coupling to the rollup

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: '6/6 complete' is the strongest trust signal in the product and it is currently a row-existence counter. A run consisting of six honest evidence-gap stubs would still display 6/6 Done and persist to the profile.
findings.smallestFix: Add an aggregate honesty column (e.g. research_artifacts.worst_verification_tier) computed in the same roll_up_research_artifact pass (min/max over children's verification_tier), exposed through audit-state. Do NOT change the ratchet or the 6/6 count — runPostCommitProfileFanout's claim CAS and the paid-media six-complete dispatch (run-lab-section/route.ts:340-395, six-sections-complete.ts:16-32) both depend on those exact semantics. Blast radius: one migration + audit-state projection; tests supabase-run-store.test.ts and six-sections-complete.test.ts pin current rollup reads.

findings.defect: Three divergent 'confidence' numbers per section, and the tier is derived from the most optimistic one. The trust-corrected confidence (computedTrust) is silently dropped from the envelope's top-level confidence whenever there is no shortfall object, and it never reaches the DB tier at all.
findings.rootCause: (a) buildEnvelope seeds confidence = raw verified share via deriveGroundedConfidence(verification) with no shortfall (run-section.ts:1016-1022; evidence-support.ts:1243-1260 — returns verifiedCount/(verified+unsupported) when shortfall lacks computedTrustConfidence). (b) annotateEvidenceSupportReview computes the real trust = min(livenessPassRate, containmentPassRate, claimSupportShare) with honest-empty(0.4)/quote-emptied(0.6) caps (deriveWave2TrustConfidence, run-section.ts:2285-2333) — but threads it into deriveGroundedConfidence ONLY via shortfall.computedTrustConfidence, and trustShortfall is built only when `shortfall !== undefined` (run-section.ts:2523-2536). When the gate produced verdict 'committable' without provenance flags, shortfall is undefined (committable-gate.ts:149-153), so the top-level confidence reverts to the raw share while computedTrust is still written into verifierSummary (run-section.ts:2631-2645). Live proof in run d838ed4e: paid-media top-level confidence 0.667 vs verifierSummary.computedTrust.confidence 0.4; BuyerICP 0.2 (trust did flow, shortfall existed) vs flag 0.625. (c) The DB tier ignores both: buildCommitPatch → buildVerificationFlag → deriveVerificationFlag computes confidence = verifiedCount/totalClaims from a.verification counts alone (commit-patch.ts:115-124; verification-tier.ts:82-107, thresholds 0.75/0.5 at lines 1-2) — liveness/containment/honest-empty never reach the tier. Concrete consequence: paid-media trust 0.4 (<0.5 ⇒ insufficient) is badged needs_review off the raw 0.667.

files.what: envelope seed confidence; wave2 trust derivation; the shortfall-only threading bug; computedTrust recorded for display regardless
deriveGroundedConfidence prefers shortfall.computedTrustConfidence else raw share
tier thresholds applied to raw verified/total only
buildVerificationFlag fed only a.verification counts + body.evidenceGap; computedTrust never consulted
live divergence: confidence 0.667 vs computedTrust.confidence 0.4 in the same artifact

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: The number the product trusts for badging is systematically the highest of the three. Paid-media's own trust math says 'insufficient' while the user-facing flag says needs_review at 0.667 — an overclaim baked in by which variable reaches which writer, not by any judgment.
findings.smallestFix: Single-writer the confidence: (1) in annotateEvidenceSupportReview, always thread trust into deriveGroundedConfidence (pass `{...(shortfall ?? emptyShortfall), computedTrustConfidence: trust.confidence}` whenever trust !== null) so envelope confidence == computedTrust; (2) in buildCommitPatch, read data.verifierSummary.computedTrust.confidence when present and clamp the flag's confidence/tier to it. Blast radius: tier distribution shifts strictly downward (more honest) — verification-tier.test.ts, commit-patch.test.ts, and run-section streaming tests pin current values; the share-snapshot and profile-insight payloads carry the flag verbatim so no shape change.

The only honesty signal in the whole screen is attached to crashes, not to weak research.

findings.defect: Every post-commit quality mechanism is advisory by construction: agentic review can only re-badge (never change status, never block), the run-level quality gate writes to a table nothing reads, and all of their failure paths are warn-and-continue.
findings.rootCause: Agentic review runs strictly AFTER commit_artifact_section returns (scheduled at supabase-run-store.ts:1281-1290, executed at 638-683); on any error attachAgenticReview returns null and logs 'keeping committed original artifact' (593-622); its re-commit uses buildReviewCommitPatch which explicitly deletes `status` from the patch (commit-patch.ts:149-162, `void status`), so the review can flip the tier (strictest-wins merge, commit-patch.ts:119-124 + verification-tier.ts:145-180) but the row stays complete. The review tier itself is model-self-reported via a <review_metadata> JSON tail (agentic-section-review.ts:517) with error fallback tiers at 483. The run-level gate evaluateLiveQualityGate is computed once the parent completes and upserted into research_quality_gate_results inside a try/catch that only console.warns (supabase-run-store.ts:555-591, invoked at 1099-1105) — and grep shows its only consumers are the writer itself and an offline script (scripts/research-quality-gate-report.ts): no API route or component reads the table. Memory's claim 'post-commit quality gates never block' is verified exact: the complete blocking set is pre-commit only (see finding 1 list), and even the paid-media verifier maps its own crash to needsReview-not-hardFail by design (run-section.ts:2662-2695 'ARI: even a verifier crash never kills the section').

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: The pipeline pays for a second model pass and a deterministic run-level gate, then discards both: the review's harshest possible outcome is an invisible tier (finding 5), and the gate verdict ('below_9_of_10_gate' etc.) is computed, persisted, and read by no one. The user pays latency and tokens for honesty signals that never reach them.
findings.smallestFix: Two cheap closures: (1) surface research_quality_gate_results.result through audit-state (or the profile page) so the already-computed run verdict is visible; (2) make agentic-review failure stamp tier 'unavailable' onto the row (today a failed review leaves the deterministic tier with no marker that review never ran). Keep everything non-blocking — that is recorded ARI design, and supabase-run-store.test.ts pins the swallow-and-keep behavior.

findings.defect: Executive brief auto-generates over insufficient sections with the tiers stripped from its input, gated only by 'paid-media committed'; it is stored in research_artifacts.thesis (not an executive_brief column), and a kickoff config quirk silently disables it.
findings.rootCause: Trigger: paid-media is a capstone (isCapstoneSection, supabase-run-store.ts:1248-1251), and its saveArtifact awaits postExecutiveBriefKickoff (1328-1330). The dispatch target is derived by string-replacing 'review-section'→'executive-brief' on the reviewDispatch URL (753-768) — if reviewDispatch is absent or its URL doesn't end in review-section, the brief is silently skipped (779-785 logs info only). The route (executive-brief/route.ts) authenticates with x-internal-key == RAILWAY_API_KEY (330-341), ACKs 202, and runs in after() (361-365). It loads ALL sections with status='complete' (216-221) — which per finding 1 is every committed section regardless of tier — and toBriefSectionInput keeps only body/title/statusSummary/verdict (104-123), discarding confidence, verification counts, tier, and review, so the brief model literally cannot know MarketCategory was 46% supported. Quality gates inside runExecutiveBrief (executive-brief.ts:850-917): refuse on zero sections (853-857), blockedBrief on unresolved critical contradictions (864-872), and guardBriefNumbers numeric-fidelity with exactly one rewrite retry, after which it SHIPS with fidelityStrikes recorded (874-898, 913) — strikes are stored in thesis, not surfaced as a block. Model = sectionWriterModel (executive-brief.ts:825; models.ts:710-712 — resolves per LAB_ENGINE_PROVIDER, deepseek-direct in prod); prompt = briefInstructions + buildBriefPrompt over the seven bodies + fact ledger + contradictions + feasibility audit. Output written to research_artifacts.thesis via writeThesis (route 195-208), status generating→complete/error (210-213, 284-300, 321-326); errors never propagate (best-effort by design, route comment 31-37).

findings.howItCreatesBadExperience: The most authoritative artifact in the deliverable — an executive decision memo — is confidently penned over sections the pipeline itself graded insufficient, with no hedging possible because the tiers were removed from its context. findings.smallestFix: Extend ExecutiveBriefSectionInput with tier+confidence (read verification_tier in the route's select, thread through toBriefSectionInput) and add one instruction line requiring the memo to mark decisions resting on needs_review/insufficient sections as 'directional'.
